title: Error when removing current change
description: Some VCS operations can remove the current change - abandon is an obvious one, but this can also happen when creating changes that cause the current (empty) change to be discarded. When this happens, the commit detail pane tries to refresh itself, causing the JujutsuFullCommitDetails to reload, but this reloading fails because the underlying change has gone. We need to change this situation to be expected, and defensively paint the commit details pane as if no commit has been selected in that situation. Ideally, the operation would already have refreshed the 'current selection' but this may happen later. As part of this fix, investigate sequencing around that to see if there is a neater way - i.e. the table realises that its current selection has disappeared, then clears the detail pane. Or: the detail pane realises that the current change has disappeared and sends a message to select the working copy in the table instead.

title: Comprehensive testing of custom log UI
Allow to initialise a jj root
description:
## Problem

The plugin needs to gracefully handle the case where it's used in a directory that is not a Jujutsu repository (no .jj directory found).

## Current Behavior

- JujutsuVcs has a `root` property that auto-discovers .jj directory by searching upward
- Unclear what happens if no .jj directory is found in the project or any parent directories

## Expected Behavior

The plugin should:
1. Detect when no JJ repository is present
2. Either disable itself gracefully or show a helpful message
3. Not throw errors or crash
4. Not show the Jujutsu tool window if not applicable

## Testing Scenarios

- [ ] Open project without .jj directory
- [ ] Check if VCS is properly disabled
- [ ] Verify no errors in IDE logs
- [ ] Ensure Jujutsu tool window doesn't appear
- [ ] Test with project that has Git but not JJ

## Related Code

- `JujutsuVcs.root` property (auto-discovery logic)
- `JujutsuRootChecker` (currently disabled due to API changes)
- VCS detection and initialization
notes:

- Logs at INFO level (user error, not plugin error)

title: Plugin hijacks global ENTER keybinding

Removing the ENTER binding from plugin.xml restores correct behaviour. title: Crash when PATH contains invalid entries (e.g. Dev:\path)
description:
Plugin crashes on startup when the system PATH contains invalid path entries (e.g. 'Dev:\sdks\flutter\' with a colon after a drive-like prefix). Reported by external user on GitHub issue #21.

Root cause: InstallMethod.kt line 12 calls Paths.get() on each PATH entry without guarding against InvalidPathException. The ExceptionInInitializerError propagates up through JjExecutableFinder and crashes the availability check on startup.

Fix: Wrap path parsing in InstallMethod.kt (getPaths / InstallMethod$Scoop) with a try/catch on InvalidPathException (and IllegalArgumentException) and skip invalid entries silently.

Secondary suggestion from reporter: check for jj already on PATH before attempting Scoop detection.

GitHub issue: https://github.com/kkkev/jj-idea/issues/21
close_reason: Fixed: wrap Paths.get() in .paths extension with try/catch, logging a warning and skipping invalid entries. Added test using null-byte path.

title: Test plugin against jj 0.40.0 and fill contract test gaps
description:
A user reporting issue #19 is on jj 0.40.0 (we've only tested against 0.37.0). The regression they hit turned out to be a bug in our code rather than a jj format change, but we should verify our contract tests all pass on 0.40.0 and add tests for any uncovered output-parsing commands so we catch future format changes early.

## What needs doing

### 1. Run existing contract CLI tests against jj 0.40.0
Install jj 0.40.0 locally (e.g. via `cargo install` or a release binary) and run `./gradlew test`. The CLI tests are annotated with `@RequiresJj` and run automatically when jj is on the PATH. Confirm all pass.

### 2. Add contract tests for three uncovered output-parsing commands
These commands produce output that the plugin parses, but have no contract tests yet:

- **`jj diff --git`** — used in `ChangeService.detectRenames()` / `parseGitDiffRenames()`. The plugin parses 'diff --git a/old b/new', 'rename from old', 'rename to new' lines. Add a `DiffGitContractTest` with rename, add, delete, and modify cases.
- **`jj config list` / `jj config get`** — used to fetch user name and email at startup. Parse each returned line as `key = value`. Add a `ConfigContractTest` verifying the format and that known keys are present.
- **`jj git remote list`** — used in `JujutsuRepositoryImpl.gitRemotesFuture` to populate the remote list. Parsed line-by-line as 'name url'. Add a `GitRemoteContractTest` using a local bare git repo as the remote.

### 3. Consider pinning jj in CI
Contract CLI tests are currently skipped in CI because jj is not installed. Consider adding a CI step to install a pinned jj version so the contract tests run on every push.
notes:
## Changelog analysis: 0.37.0 → 0.40.0

### No confirmed breakage in output formats we parse

**0.40.0: `diff.git.show-path-prefix` config** — new option suppresses `a/`/`b/` prefixes in git diff path lines. Our `parseGitDiffRenames()` only reads `rename from` and `rename to` lines, which never carry those prefixes. Not a risk.

**0.38.0: `all:` revset modifier removed** — we use `all()` (function form), never `all:`. Confirmed by grep. Not a risk.

**0.38.0: `jj git init --colocate` now refuses to run inside Git worktrees** — contract tests use @TempDir which is outside the project tree. Not a risk.

**0.38.0: Legacy user.name/email placeholder removed** — only affects users who never set these in jj config, in which case jj itself would refuse most commands. Not our problem.

### Items to verify when running against 0.40.0

**0.39.0: `jj new` evaluates `new_description` template** — if the user has this template configured, calling `jj new` without `--message` will produce a non-empty description. Our code omits `--message` when description is empty. This doesn't break output parsing but could affect UX: new changes would arrive pre-described from the template. The contract test only checks change ID, not description, so it won't catch this.

**0.38.0: `<name>@<remote>` now resolves to remote tags (prioritised over bookmarks)** — we display and resolve remote bookmark refs like `master@origin`. If a repo has a remote tag with the same name as a remote bookmark, revset lookups could resolve to the wrong thing. Edge case, but worth a conscious look when testing against 0.40.0.

### Opportunity noticed

**0.38.0: `git_web_url([remote])` template function** — converts a Git remote URL to its web URL (github.com/…, gitlab.com/…). We currently do this manually in OpenInRemoteFromEditorGroup with regex. Worth considering replacing our URL parser with this template call in a future cleanup, since jj's implementation will track edge cases we might miss.

title: Fix 'Configure VCS roots' link navigating to wrong settings page
description:
The 'Configure VCS roots...' hyperlink in the empty working copy panel used the wrong configurable ID ('project.propVCSSupport.DirectoryMappings' instead of 'project.propVCSSupport.Mappings'). ShowSettingsUtil silently falls back to the last-visited settings page when the ID isn't found, which is exactly what the reporter in GitHub issue #20 experienced on WebStorm.

Also extracted the magic string into a Project.showVcsMappingsSettings() extension function in SettingsNavigation.kt so there is a single Kotlin source of truth for the ID.

Ref: https://github.com/kkkev/jj-idea/issues/20

title: Regression: Local Changes always empty, gutter changes disappear on save
description: GitHub Issue #19. After a plugin update, editing a file shows changes in the gutter, but as soon as the file is saved it shows as unchanged again and Local Changes are always empty. This is a regression — it worked before the update.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED (from user's log snapshot):

In parseStatusLine(), the VirtualFile lookup for M and A files:
  val file = repo.directory.findFileByRelativePath(filePath)

was used as a null-check gate before calling addModifiedChange/addAddedChange, even though file was never passed to those methods. findFileByRelativePath explicitly 'does not perform refresh' — it only reads IntelliJ's in-memory VFS cache. In the user's large multi-module Go workspace (fresh project open, VFS still scanning), subdirectory files weren't in the cache yet when getChanges fired. So M and A were silently dropped.

Deleted files (D) had no such guard — that's why they always appeared.

The fix: removed the if (file != null) guard and the findFileByRelativePath lookup entirely from M and A cases. path from getChildPath() is sufficient and always works. Also removed the test 'add non-existent file silently ignores' that encoded the wrong behaviour.

Colocated repo was not the direct cause; VFS cache timing affects any large project. The contract tests already cover colocated repos and pass.
close_reason: Fixed: removed spurious if (file != null) guard from M and A cases in JujutsuChangeProvider.parseStatusLine(). The VirtualFile lookup (findFileByRelativePath, no-refresh) was failing in large projects where VFS scanning is still in progress, silently dropping modified and added files. Deleted files worked because they had no such guard.

title: Run jj commands in repo context for scope-aware config
description: GitHub #9: jj config commands (like 'config get user.email') run in home directory instead of repo directory, breaking [[--scope]] config. All jj commands should run in repo context (via working directory or -R flag) so scoped config works correctly. See https://github.com/kkkev/jj-idea/issues/9
design:
# Plan: Run jj config commands in repo context (jj-idea-euro)

## Context

GitHub #9: `jj config get` and `jj config set` run with `null` working directory (home dir), so repo-scoped jj config is never picked up. The user config checker runs once globally on startup with a rootless executor. Users need a permanent way to view/edit identity config (not just the one-time notification), and the settings UI must handle multi-repo projects with extensible per-repo overrides.

## Changes

### 1. Fix `configGet`/`configSet` to run in repo context

**File**: `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/jj/cli/CliExecutor.kt`

- `configGet`: change `execute(null, ...)` to `execute(root, ...)` so repo-scoped config is resolved when executor has a root
- Add `ConfigScope` enum (`USER`/`REPO`) to `CommandExecutor.kt`
- Add `configSet(key, value, scope)` method alongside existing `configSetUser` (which becomes a delegate)
- `configSet` implementation: `execute(root, listOf("config", "set", scope.flag, key, value))`

### 2. Per-repo user config checking on startup

**File**: `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/services/JujutsuStartupActivity.kt`

Instead of checking once with a rootless executor, subscribe to `stateModel.initializedRoots` and check each repo's config using its own `commandExecutor`. Track notified repo paths to avoid spam. `JjUserConfigChecker` already takes a `CommandExecutor` — just pass a repo-bound one.

### 3. Settings UI: User Identity + Repository Settings sections

**File**: `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/settings/JujutsuConfigurable.kt`

Redesign the settings page layout:

```
[Executable]              (existing, unchanged)
[Installation Help]       (existing, unchanged)

[User Identity]           << NEW
  Name:  [___________]
  Email: [___________]
  (comment: "Saved to jj user config (~/.jjconfig.toml)")

[UI Preferences]          (existing, unchanged)

[Default Log Settings]    (existing, renamed from "Log Settings")
  Changes to show: [500]

[Repository Settings]     << NEW (hidden when no repos initialized)
  Repository: [▼ my-repo       ]
  □ Override user identity
      Name:  [___________]
      Email: [___________]
  □ Override log change limit: [___]
```

**Behavior:**
- User Identity section loads global values via `jj config get` on background thread when panel opens, saves via `jj config set --user` on apply
- Repository Settings uses a `ComboBox` populated from `stateModel.initializedRoots.value`
- Each override has a checkbox; unchecked = disabled fields showing inherited value as placeholder
- User identity overrides save via `jj config set --repo` (needs repo-bound executor)
- Log change limit overrides save to existing `RepositoryConfig` in `JujutsuSettingsState.repositoryOverrides`
- Switching repo in ComboBox loads that repo's current override state
- Extensible: adding future per-repo settings (e.g., log revset) = add another checkbox + field

**State management:**
- On panel open: load global identity (background), load per-repo identity for first repo (background), load per-repo plugin settings from `RepositoryConfig`
- On repo ComboBox change: load that repo's identity (background), load its `RepositoryConfig`
- On apply: save global identity if changed, save repo overrides for all modified repos

### 4. Update `JjUserConfigChecker` and dialog

**File**: `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/setup/JjUserConfigChecker.kt`
- `setConfig` accepts `ConfigScope` parameter

**File**: `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/setup/JjUserConfigDialog.kt`
- Add scope radio button (Global / This Repository) with repo name shown
- Pass scope through to `setConfig`

**File**: `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/services/JujutsuNotifications.kt`
- `notifyUserConfigNeeded` accepts repo info for per-repo notification message
- Replace `userConfigNotified: Boolean` with `Set<String>` of notified repo paths

### 5. Bundle messages

**File**: `src/main/resources/messages/JujutsuBundle.properties`

New keys for: user identity group/labels, repository settings group/labels, override checkboxes, scope radio labels, per-repo notification content.

## Files to modify

1. `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/jj/CommandExecutor.kt` — `ConfigScope` enum, `configSet` method
2. `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/jj/cli/CliExecutor.kt` — `configSet` impl, fix `configGet` working dir
3. `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/settings/JujutsuConfigurable.kt` — User Identity + Repository Settings sections
4. `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/setup/JjUserConfigChecker.kt` — scope parameter
5. `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/setup/JjUserConfigDialog.kt` — scope radio, repo display
6. `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/services/JujutsuNotifications.kt` — per-repo tracking, scope
7. `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/services/JujutsuStartupActivity.kt` — per-repo checking
8. `src/main/resources/messages/JujutsuBundle.properties` — new message keys

## Verification

1. `./gradlew check` — build + tests pass
2. `./gradlew runIde`:
   - Set repo-scoped config: `jj config set --repo user.name "RepoUser"` → IDE should not prompt for config
   - Open Settings → Jujutsu → User Identity shows global values
   - Repository Settings dropdown shows all repos, override checkboxes work
   - Save identity with repo scope → `jj config list --repo` shows values
   - Multi-repo project: each repo shows its own overrides independently
close_reason: Implemented: per-repo config checking on startup, scope radio buttons in user config dialog, User Identity group in settings, Repository Settings group with per-repo identity and log limit overrides

title: Refactor: Consolidate state model & messaging
description:
The state model (JujutsuStateModel) and messaging infrastructure (Messaging.kt) have grown organically, with different requirements pulling them in different directions. This task consolidates event handling, eliminates redundant work, and documents the system.

## Problems

1. **IDE event connections in wrong places**: VCS_CONFIGURATION_CHANGED and VCS_ACTIVATED subscriptions live in ToolWindowEnabler rather than the state model they feed
2. **Wasted initial load**: SimpleNotifiableState auto-invalidates on construction, causing repositoryStates to load an empty set (initializedRoots hasn't loaded yet), then double-invalidates initializedRoots
3. **logRefresh fires twice per VCS operation**: Once from repo.invalidate(), once from repositoryStates cascade handler
4. **Duplicate tool window availability setting**: Both JujutsuStateModel and ToolWindowEnabler set it
5. **Misleading startup activity name**: ToolWindowEnablerInitActivity actually bootstraps the entire state model
6. **No documentation**: No docs explain the architecture, threading contracts, or cascade design

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Messaging.kt — Remove auto-invalidation
Remove init { invalidate() } from SimpleNotifiableState. Callers must call invalidate() explicitly. This eliminates wasted initial load of repositoryStates and double invalidation of initializedRoots. Add KDoc documenting threading contract, versioning, and explicit initialization requirement.

### Step 2: JujutsuStateModel.kt — Consolidate IDE events, fix cascades
- 2a. Move VCS_CONFIGURATION_CHANGED and VCS_ACTIVATED subscriptions here from ToolWindowEnabler
- 2b. Remove tool window availability from initializedRoots handler (duplicate of ToolWindowEnabler). Remove ToolWindowManager and WorkingCopyToolWindowFactory imports (eliminates jj/ → ui/ dependency)
- 2c. Fix logRefresh double-firing: Remove logRefresh.notify() from repositoryStates handler. Add it to debounce collector and initializedRoots handler instead. Result: logRefresh fires from exactly 3 non-overlapping sources: repo.invalidate() (VCS ops), debounce collector (file changes), initializedRoots handler (root changes)
- 2d. Remove redundant coroutineScope.launch wrapper around initializedRoots.invalidate() (invalidate() is already non-blocking)
- 2e. Add refresh suppression hooks (prep for bead jj-idea-q3lk): AtomicInteger counter, suppressRefresh()/resumeRefresh() methods, guard in BulkFileListener. ~15 lines, zero cost when unused
- 2f. Add comprehensive KDoc with architecture diagram, threading contract, initialization order

### Step 3: ToolWindowEnabler.kt — Remove IDE event subscriptions
Remove VCS_CONFIGURATION_CHANGED, VCS_ACTIVATED subscriptions and TODO comment. Init becomes just: initializedRoots.connect(coroutineScope) { handleRootsChange(it) }

### Step 4: Rename startup activity
ToolWindowEnablerInitActivity.kt → JujutsuStartupActivity.kt. Update KDoc to explain bootstrap chain. Body unchanged.

### Step 5: plugin.xml
Update postStartupActivity class reference.

### Step 6: CLAUDE.md
Update state model documentation section.

## Event Flow After Refactoring

IDE Events (all subscribed in JujutsuStateModel):
- VFS_CHANGES (.jj create/delete), VCS_CONFIGURATION_CHANGED, VCS_ACTIVATED → initializedRoots.invalidate()
  - → repositoryStates.invalidate() + logRefresh.notify() + ToolWindowEnabler
- VFS_CHANGES (file content) → 300ms debounce → repositoryStates.invalidate() + logRefresh.notify()
  - repositoryStates loaded → UnifiedWorkingCopyPanel + VcsDirtyScopeManager
- repo.invalidate(select) (VCS ops) → repositoryStates.invalidate() + logRefresh.notify() + changeSelection.notify()

## Analysis: Notifiable States
- initializedRoots: Fast loader (fs metadata), rare changes, no debounce needed, versioning kept (harmless)
- repositoryStates: Moderate loader (jj log external process), frequent changes, 300ms debounce, versioning needed
- logRefresh/changeSelection: Fire-and-forget Notifiers, no state/dedup needed, BackgroundDataLoader coalesces in UI

## Race Condition Analysis
All races benign with current primitives (version counter, @Volatile, AtomicInteger). No additional synchronization needed at current complexity.

## Verification
- ./gradlew check (compilation, tests, ktlint)
- Manual: open/close project, add/remove VCS mapping, file edits, VCS operations
- Check startup logs for single initializedRoots load (not double), single logRefresh per operation (not double)

## Files to Modify
1. src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/jj/util/Messaging.kt
2. src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/jj/JujutsuStateModel.kt
3. src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/services/ToolWindowEnabler.kt
4. src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/services/ToolWindowEnablerInitActivity.kt → JujutsuStartupActivity.kt
5. src/main/resources/META-INF/plugin.xml
6. CLAUDE.md
close_reason: All steps complete: removed auto-invalidation from SimpleNotifiableState, consolidated IDE event subscriptions into JujutsuStateModel, fixed logRefresh double-firing, removed tool window availability duplication, added refresh suppression hooks, added comprehensive KDoc to both Messaging.kt and JujutsuStateModel.kt, cleaned up ToolWindowEnabler unused imports, renamed ToolWindowEnablerInitActivity to JujutsuStartupActivity, updated plugin.xml and CLAUDE.md

title: Fix plugin verifier warnings and compatibility problem
description:
The IntelliJ Plugin Verifier reports issues across 2025.1/2025.2/2025.3. One is a real compatibility break; the rest are API usage warnings that should be addressed for marketplace quality.

## Compatibility Problem (breaks 2025.1)
- `IconUtil.colorize$default` in `IconResolver.resolveIcon()` (`IconAwareHtmlPane.kt:38`) — the `colorize` signature changed in 2025.2 (added `keepBrightness` param). The existing `catch (NoSuchMethodError)` handles runtime, but the verifier flags the unresolved bytecode reference. Fix: use reflection, or bump `sinceBuild` to 252.

## Deprecated API (3 usages)
1. `ActionUtil.invokeAction` in `JujutsuChangesTree.installHandlers`
2. `Disposer.isDisposed` in `SimpleNotifiableState.connect`
3. `VcsFileContent.getContent()` overridden in `JujutsuFileRevision`

## Internal API (17 usages, grouped)
1. `SelectedBlockHistoryAction` (4 refs) in `ShowFileHistoryForSelectionAction` — constructor, class refs, field
2. `ActionToolbarImpl` (6 refs) in `CommitTablePanel.createFilterField` — constructor, class refs, method overrides
3. `CheckboxIcon.createAndScaleCheckbox` (2 refs) in `JujutsuRootFilterComponent.ToggleRootAction`
4. `AnAction.setShortcutSet` in `OpenChangeFileAction`
5. `AppLifecycleListener.appStarted` overridden in `JujutsuFileTypeSetup`
6. `StartupUiUtil.isDarkTheme` in `JujutsuFilterComponent.setForeground`
7. `RefreshVFsSynchronously.refreshVirtualFilesRecursive` in `InitAction` (marked internal in 2025.2+)

## Override-only Violation (1)
- `AnAction.actionPerformed` invoked directly in `JujutsuNotifications.notifyUninitializedRoot` — should only be overridden, not called

## Experimental API (19 usages — informational, likely acceptable)
- Mostly `JBHtmlPane` / `JBHtmlPaneConfiguration` (no public alternative exists)
- `CacheableAnnotationProvider` (needed for annotation caching)

## Verification was run against
- IC-251.29188.11 (IntelliJ Community 2025.1)
- IC-252.28539.13 (IntelliJ Community 2025.2)
- IU-253.31033.145 (IntelliJ Ultimate 2025.3, marketplace)

## Configuration added
`pluginVerification { ides { recommended() } }` block added to `build.gradle.kts` — run `./gradlew verifyPlugin` to reproduce.
close_reason: Fixed all addressable plugin verifier warnings: replaced IconUtil.colorize with custom SVGs + own colorize, replaced deprecated/internal APIs (ActionUtil.invokeAction, StartupUiUtil.isDarkTheme, RefreshVFsSynchronously, CheckboxIcon, SelectedBlockHistoryAction, setShortcutSet, AppLifecycleListener), fixed @OverrideOnly violation, removed redundant @Service registrations from plugin.xml, bumped sinceBuild to 252. Remaining: 2 deprecated (unavoidable), 6 internal (ActionToolbarImpl, no public alternative), 19 experimental (expected).

title: Add integration tests using jj stub
description: Write integration tests that verify plugin behaviour (CliExecutor, CliLogService, ChangeProvider, AnnotationParser, etc.) against the jj stub. These tests exercise the full parsing pipeline end-to-end without needing a real jj installation or IntelliJ platform, giving fast deterministic feedback on plugin correctness.
design:
# Integration Tests Using jj Stub (jj-idea-sljq)

## Context

The project has contract tests that verify raw CLI output format (field counts, separators) against both real jj and the in-memory stub, and unit tests that test parsers with hand-crafted strings. The missing layer is **integration tests** that exercise the full parsing pipeline end-to-end: `JjStub` → `CommandExecutor` → `CliLogService`/`AnnotationParser` → parsed domain objects (`LogEntry`, `FileChange`, `BookmarkItem`, `AnnotationLine`). These catch mismatches between template generation and output parsing without needing a real jj installation or IntelliJ platform.

## Approach

### 1. Create `StubCommandExecutor` adapter

**New file**: `src/test/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/contract/StubCommandExecutor.kt`

Wraps `JjStub` as a `CommandExecutor`, translating between the typed interface and raw `JjBackend.run()` calls. Only methods needed by `CliLogService` and annotation tests need real implementations; others throw `UnsupportedOperationException`.

Methods to implement:
- `log(revset, template, filePaths, limit)` → `stub.run("log", "-r", revset, "--no-graph", "-T", template)`
- `diffSummary(revision)` → `stub.run("diff", "--summary", "-r", revision)`
- `bookmarkList(template)` → `stub.run("bookmark", "list", "-T", template)`
- `annotate(file, revision, template)` → `stub.run("file", "annotate", "-r", revision, "-T", template, file.path)`
- `status()` → `stub.run("status")`
- All other methods: `TODO("Not needed for integration tests")`

Convert `JjBackend.Result` → `CommandExecutor.CommandResult` via simple mapping.

### 2. Update `JjStub.cmdBookmarkList()` for 3-field template

**Modify**: `src/test/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/contract/JjStub.kt`

The actual `bookmarkListTemplate` in `CliLogService` generates a 3-field template (`present`, `nameWithRemote`, `qualifiedChangeId`), but the stub currently outputs only 2 fields. Update `cmdBookmarkList()` to detect the full template (contains `"present"`) and output 3 fields:
- `field("true")` (all stub bookmarks are present/local)
- `field(name)` (no remotes in stub, so `nameWithRemote` = name)
- `field(qualifiedChangeId(change))`

Keep backward compat with the 2-field template used by `BookmarkContractTest`.

### 3. Create integration test classes

**New file**: `src/test/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/integration/LogServiceIntegrationTest.kt`

Tagged `@Tag("stub")` — runs in both `./gradlew test` and `./gradlew stubTest`, no jj required.

Setup pattern:
```kotlin
val stub = JjStub(tempDir)
val executor = StubCommandExecutor(stub)
val repo = mockk<JujutsuRepository> { every { commandExecutor } returns executor }
val logService = CliLogService(repo)
```

**Test cases for `getLogBasic()`:**
- Single entry with description → verify changeId, commitId, description, isWorkingCopy=true
- Multiple entries → verify count and ordering
- Entry with bookmarks → verify bookmarks list
- Multiline description → verify preserved correctly
- Empty working copy → verify isEmpty=true

**Test cases for `getLog()` (full):**
- Verify author name, email, and timestamps populated
- Verify committer fields populated

**Test cases for `getFileChanges()`:**
- Added file → ADDED status
- Modified file → MODIFIED status
- Deleted file → DELETED status
- Mixed changes → correct statuses for each

**Test cases for `getBookmarks()`:**
- Single bookmark → name and changeId
- Multiple bookmarks → all returned

**New file**: `src/test/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/integration/AnnotationIntegrationTest.kt`

Tests the annotation pipeline: `CommandExecutor.annotate()` → `AnnotationParser.parse()`.

**Test cases:**
- Single-change file → all lines attributed to same change
- Multi-change file → lines attributed to different changes
- Verify parsed AnnotationLine fields: changeId, commitId, author, timestamp, description, lineContent, lineNumber

### Files to create/modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `src/test/kotlin/.../contract/StubCommandExecutor.kt` | Create |
| `src/test/kotlin/.../contract/JjStub.kt` | Modify (3-field bookmark template) |
| `src/test/kotlin/.../integration/LogServiceIntegrationTest.kt` | Create |
| `src/test/kotlin/.../integration/AnnotationIntegrationTest.kt` | Create |

### Key reuse points

- `CliLogService(repo)` — the class under test (`src/main/kotlin/.../jj/cli/CliLogService.kt`)
- `AnnotationParser.parse()` / `AnnotationParser.TEMPLATE` (`src/main/kotlin/.../jj/cli/AnnotationParser.kt`)
- `JjStub` (`src/test/kotlin/.../contract/JjStub.kt`) — the in-memory backend
- `JjBackend` (`src/test/kotlin/.../contract/JjBackend.kt`) — backend interface
- MockK for `JujutsuRepository` (already a test dependency)
- `@Tag("stub")` for test classification (existing pattern)

## Verification

```bash
./gradlew test          # Integration tests run here (tagged "stub", not excluded)
./gradlew stubTest      # Also runs here (includes "stub" tag)
./gradlew check         # Full check including tests
```

title: Add logRefresh notifier to decouple log from repositoryStates
description:
Currently `repositoryStates` is used as a proxy for 'any log change'. A proper 'log changed' state would be semantically correct and allow finer-grained refresh.

**Context**: The log panel listens to `repositoryStates` changes, but this is actually a working copy/repository state signal. When we want to refresh the log specifically (e.g., after operations that don't change working copy status), we don't have a proper semantic signal.

**Proposed solution**:
- Add a `logChanged` notifiable state to JujutsuStateModel
- JujutsuLogPanel subscribes to this instead of repositoryStates
- Operations that affect the log (new, edit, abandon, describe, etc.) invalidate logChanged
- Operations that only affect working copy files invalidate repositoryStates

**Benefits**:
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Allows finer-grained refresh
- More semantic state model

**Related**: This emerged from jj-idea-850 during the workingCopies → repositoryStates rename.
close_reason: Added logRefresh notifier to decouple log panel from repositoryStates. Log now refreshes after all VCS operations including non-WC commit operations like abandon.

title: Fix disposal error when opening native log pane in mixed VCS project
description:
When trying to open a native log pane in the VCS tool window for a mixed VCS root project, an IncorrectOperationException is thrown because the parent VcsLogUiImpl has already been disposed.

The root cause is in ToolWindowEnabler.kt:100 - the contentAdded listener removes content immediately, causing the VcsLogUiImpl to be disposed before VcsLogContentUtil.openLogTab finishes initializing it.

Stack trace shows:
1. OpenAnotherLogTabAction.actionPerformed triggers
2. VcsLogContentUtil.openLogTab adds content
3. ContentManagerImpl.fireContentAdded triggers our listener
4. ToolWindowEnabler.contentAdded removes the content (disposing VcsLogUiImpl)
5. VcsLogContentUtil.openLogTab tries to register a child disposable on the already-disposed parent
6. IncorrectOperationException is thrown

The default log tab suppressor is too aggressive - it removes content synchronously in the contentAdded callback, which interferes with the platform's initialization sequence.

Potential fix: Defer the removal with invokeLater, or check if the content is fully initialized before removing, or use a different approach to suppress default log tabs.

title: Verify threading correctness in JujutsuCompareWithBranchAction
description:
**File**: JujutsuCompareWithBranchAction.kt:35

**Problem**: There is a TODO questioning whether the threading is correct. The code calls `invokeLater` from the action's `actionPerformed` which runs on BGT (Background Thread) since the action has `getActionUpdateThread() = ActionUpdateThread.BGT`.

**Analysis needed**:
1. Is calling `invokeLater` from BGT correct? (Should be fine - invokeLater schedules on EDT)
2. The popup `JujutsuCompareWithPopup.show()` needs to run on EDT (which `invokeLater` ensures)
3. The diff content loading correctly uses `executeOnPooledThread` then `invokeLater` for EDT

**Likely resolution**: The threading appears correct - `invokeLater` is the right way to get onto EDT from BGT. The TODO can probably be removed after verification.

description:
Lines don't continue through intermediate cells when parent/child are separated

**Problem**: Major rendering bug - lines disappear when commits aren't in consecutive rows

**Current behavior**:
- Commit A (row 0, lane 0) has child
- Commit B (row 1, lane 1) - different branch  
- Commit C (row 2, lane 0) - parent of A
- Line from A stops, doesn't reach C

**Root cause**: 
Each cell only draws to immediately adjacent rows. Need to track which lanes are 'active' and draw pass-through lines in intermediate cells.

**Solution**:
Track active lanes at each row, draw continuation lines for lanes that pass through but don't have commits

**Complexity**: High - requires redesigning how graph data flows to renderer

title: Investigate VCS log internal API accessibility and alternatives
description:
## Investigation: Option B - Fully Custom Log UI

**Decision**: Build completely custom table/graph UI from scratch, no VcsLogUiEx dependency.

## Architecture: Standalone Custom Log UI

### Core Principle
Don't extend IntelliJ's VcsLogUi at all. Build our own UI that:
- Uses our JujutsuLogProvider data directly
- Has its own table component
- Renders its own commit graph
- Lives in our own tool window tab

### Components to Build

#### 1. Data Layer (Reuse existing)
✅ Already have:
- JujutsuLogProvider - provides commits
- LogService - gets log entries
- ChangeId, LogEntry - domain types

#### 2. Table Component (Build from scratch)
**JujutsuLogTable** - Custom JTable
- Extends JTable directly (not VcsLogGraphTable)
- Custom TableModel with our columns
- No dependency on GraphTableModel
- Columns:
  - Graph (visual commit graph)
  - Status (conflict/empty icons)
  - Change ID (formatted)
  - Description (with right-aligned refs)
  - Author
  - Date

#### 3. Graph Rendering (Build custom)
**JujutsuCommitGraphRenderer** - Custom cell renderer
- Draw commit graph using Graphics2D
- Circles for commits
- Lines for parent relationships
- Different colors for branches
- Similar to VcsLogGraphTable but simpler

#### 4. Column Renderers (Custom implementations)
- **GraphCellRenderer** - Draws commit graph
- **StatusCellRenderer** - Conflict/empty icons
- **ChangeIdCellRenderer** - Bold prefix + gray remainder
- **DescriptionCellRenderer** - Description + right-aligned bookmark icons
- **AuthorCellRenderer** - Author name
- **DateCellRenderer** - Relative dates

#### 5. Main Panel (Custom layout)
**JujutsuLogPanel** - Main UI component
- JujutsuLogTable in scroll pane
- Toolbar (refresh, filters)
- Details pane (commit info when selected)
- Status bar (commit count, etc.)

#### 6. Data Loading (Custom)
**JujutsuLogDataLoader** - Background loading
- Load commits from JujutsuLogProvider
- Build graph structure from parent relationships
- Update table model on EDT
- Handle pagination/incremental loading

### Integration Points

**Instead of VcsLogUi registration**:
- Add tab to our existing 'Working copy' tool window
- Or create new 'Jujutsu' tool window with multiple tabs
- Use ContentFactory to create tabs

**Data source**:
- JujutsuLogProvider (already works)
- Call logService.getLog() directly
- No dependency on VcsLogData/VcsLogManager

**Actions**:
- Right-click context menu with our own actions
- Double-click to show diff
- Keyboard shortcuts (F4, Enter, etc.)

## Advantages of This Approach

1. **No internal API dependencies** - Only use public IntelliJ SDK
2. **Complete control** - Every pixel is ours
3. **Simpler codebase** - No fighting with platform abstractions
4. **Jujutsu-native** - Design specifically for JJ concepts
5. **Easier to maintain** - No platform update breakage
6. **Faster iteration** - No reverse-engineering platform behavior

## Challenges

1. **Graph algorithm** - Need to implement commit graph layout
2. **Performance** - Optimize for large repos (1000+ commits)
3. **Polish** - Match platform look & feel
4. **Features** - Implement filtering, search, etc.
5. **Effort** - More code to write upfront

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Basic Table (jj-idea-9to, jj-idea-jbb, jj-idea-57u)
- JujutsuLogTable with basic columns (no graph yet)
- Simple TableModel loading from LogService
- Basic rendering (text only)
- Show in tool window tab
**Goal**: See commits in a table

### Phase 2: Graph Rendering (new task)
- Implement commit graph layout algorithm
- GraphCellRenderer with Graphics2D
- Parent relationship visualization
**Goal**: Visual commit graph

### Phase 3: Custom Renderers (jj-idea-53v, jj-idea-aq7)
- All column-specific renderers
- Icons, colors, formatting
- Right-aligned refs in description
**Goal**: Professional appearance

### Phase 4: Features (jj-idea-9y2, jj-idea-ncg, jj-idea-6gx)
- Details pane
- Filtering/search
- Context menu actions
**Goal**: Feature parity with needs

### Phase 5: Polish (jj-idea-qvf, jj-idea-035)
- Performance optimization
- Keyboard shortcuts
- Testing
**Goal**: Production ready

## References for Graph Algorithm

- Git log --graph implementation
- Gitk graph rendering
- IntelliJ's graph (for inspiration, not code reuse)

## Decision: Proceed with Option B

This is more work upfront but gives us a maintainable, Jujutsu-native solution.

title: Visual polish: themes, scaling, tooltips
description:
Visual polish pass for custom log UI to ensure professional appearance across themes, display scales, and with proper tooltips.

## Issues Found

### P0 - Critical (Must Fix)

1. **Hardcoded graph colors don't adapt to themes**
   - File: `JujutsuCommitGraph.kt:51-58`
   - Issue: Uses `Color(0x...)` instead of `JBColor`
   - Impact: Poor contrast in dark theme, unprofessional appearance
   - Fix: Replace with `JBColor(lightColor, darkColor)` pairs

2. **Hardcoded graph dimensions break HiDPI**
   - File: `JujutsuGraphAndDescriptionRenderer.kt:36-39`
   - Issue: `LANE_WIDTH=16`, `ROW_HEIGHT=22`, etc. are hardcoded ints
   - Impact: Tiny graph on 4K displays, wrong spacing
   - Fix: Use `JBValue.UIInteger()` pattern (see `JujutsuLabelPainter.kt` for example)

3. **Missing status indicator tooltips**
   - Files: Both `JujutsuLogTableRenderers.kt` and `JujutsuGraphAndDescriptionRenderer.kt`
   - Issue: Conflict/Empty/Immutable icons have no explanatory tooltips
   - Impact: Users don't understand what icons mean
   - Fix: Add HTML tooltips explaining each indicator

### P1 - High Priority (Should Fix)

4. **Hardcoded padding in description renderer**
   - File: `JujutsuLogTableRenderers.kt:209`
   - Issue: `HORIZONTAL_PADDING = 4` doesn't scale
   - Fix: Use `JBValue` pattern

5. **Hardcoded filter component spacing**
   - File: `JujutsuFilterComponent.kt:37-39`
   - Issue: `GAP_BEFORE_ARROW`, `BORDER_SIZE`, `ARC_SIZE` are raw ints
   - Fix: Use `JBUI.scale()`

### P2 - Nice to Have

6. **Missing informative tooltips on columns**
   - ChangeId: Could show full hex
   - Author/Committer: Could show email address
   - Low priority, not blocking

7. **Column width scaling**
   - File: `JujutsuLogTableRenderers.kt:470-479`
   - Consider using `JBUI.scale()` for initial widths
   - Very low priority, users can resize

## Good Examples in Codebase

Reference these for correct patterns:
- **Scaling**: `JujutsuLabelPainter.kt` (perfect JBValue usage)
- **Themes**: `JujutsuColors.kt` (proper JBColor patterns)
- **Tooltips**: Description tooltips in renderers (HTML formatting)

## Testing Checklist

- [ ] Test in light theme
- [ ] Test in dark theme (Darcula)
- [ ] Test at 100% UI scale
- [ ] Test at 150% UI scale
- [ ] Test at 200% UI scale
- [ ] Verify all status tooltips show
- [ ] Check graph colors have good contrast in both themes

Track deleted file status in VcsFileRevisionEx
description:
VcsFileRevisionEx requires isDeleted() to indicate if a file was deleted in this revision.

**Critical for read accuracy**: Without delete tracking, deleted files show incorrectly in history and diff views.

Currently returns hardcoded false. Should determine actual status from LogEntry.

**Location**: JujutsuFileRevision.kt line 76

**Implementation**:
- Parse file status from jj log output (added/modified/removed)
- Check if file appears in LogEntry's file changes with deleted status
- Cache result to avoid repeated queries
- Return true only when file was actually deleted in this commit

**JJ provides**: File status in log template with `files` field

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] isDeleted() returns true for deleted files
- [ ] Deleted files marked correctly in history view
- [ ] Diff view handles deleted files properly
- [ ] No performance regression from status checks

title: Implement file rename detection in history
description:
JJ tracks file renames in its log output. We need to parse and expose this information in the file history view.

**Critical for read accuracy**: Without rename tracking, file history breaks at renames - users lose access to pre-rename history.

The getRename() method in VcsLogFileHistoryHandler should:
- Parse rename information from jj log output
- Return Rename object when a file was renamed in a commit
- Enable following file history across renames

**Location**: JujutsuLogFileHistoryHandler.kt line 114 (getRename method)

**Implementation**:
- Check if LogEntry contains rename information
- Extract old and new file paths from jj log --copies output
- Return Rename object with before/after paths
- Ensure history follows renames automatically

**JJ Command**: `jj log --copies` shows copy/rename information

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Detects renames from jj log output
- [ ] Returns Rename with old/new paths
- [ ] File history follows renames (pre-rename commits visible)
- [ ] Test with renamed files in history view

title: Implement VcsLogDiffHandler methods for comparing revisions from log
description:
Currently JujutsuLogDiffHandler has stub implementations for:
- showDiff() - Compare two revisions
- showDiffWithLocal() - Compare revision with local working copy
- showDiffForPaths() - Compare specific paths between revisions

These are needed to enable diff operations from the VCS Log view (when right-clicking commits).

**Location**: JujutsuLogProvider.kt lines 235-265

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Implement showDiff() to compare any two revisions
- [ ] Implement showDiffWithLocal() to compare revision with working copy
- [ ] Implement showDiffForPaths() for multi-file diff
- [ ] Test from VCS Log by right-clicking commits

title: File history: Open in remote (GitLab/GitHub)
design:
## Design: Open in Remote (GitHub/GitLab)

### How IntelliJ's Git Plugin Does It

The Git plugin's \"Open on GitHub\" does NOT use RepositoryLocation or VcsFileRevision.getChangedRepositoryPath(). Instead it uses a dedicated infrastructure:

- `HostedGitRepositoriesManager` discovers remotes reactively via Kotlin flows
- `GlobalHostedGitRepositoryReferenceActionGroup` provides context-aware actions
- GitHub plugin implements these interfaces and registers via `Git.Hosting.Open.In.Browser.Group` action group
- Data sources: VCS_FILE_REVISION (file history), VCS_LOG_COMMIT_SELECTION (log), VIRTUAL_FILE+EDITOR (editor)

We can't reuse this because it requires GitRepository objects from GitRepositoryManager, which won't exist for jj-registered roots.

### Proposed Approach: Simple Custom Action

Since we're a standalone VCS plugin (not a Git hosting plugin), implement a simpler self-contained approach:

**1. URL Builder** — Pure function that constructs web URLs:
```kotlin
object RemoteUrlBuilder {
    fun commitUrl(remoteUrl: String, commitHash: String): String?
    fun fileUrl(remoteUrl: String, commitHash: String, filePath: String, lineRange: IntRange? = null): String?
}
```

Supports GitHub and GitLab URL patterns by parsing the remote URL:
- SSH: `git@github.com:user/repo.git` → `https://github.com/user/repo`
- HTTPS: `https://github.com/user/repo.git` → `https://github.com/user/repo`
- Commit: `<base>/commit/<hash>`
- File: `<base>/blob/<hash>/<path>` (GitHub) or `<base>/-/blob/<hash>/<path>` (GitLab)

**2. Remote Resolution** — Use existing `CommandExecutor.gitRemoteList()`:
- Parse `<name> <url>` lines
- Prefer `origin` remote
- Cache per repository (remotes rarely change)

**3. Action Integration** — Add to existing context menus:

a) **File history** (JujutsuHistoryProvider): Add \"Open on Remote\" to `getAdditionalActions()`. Get commit hash from `JujutsuRevisionNumber` → need to expose `CommitId` from `JujutsuFileRevision` or store it in `JujutsuRevisionNumber`.

b) **Custom log** (JujutsuLogContextMenuActions): Add action that reads selected `LogEntry.commitId`.

c) **Editor/file** (optional, future): Open current file at working copy's commit on remote.

**4. Data Available Today**:
- `LogEntry.commitId: CommitId` — the git commit hash (full + short), already parsed
- `JujutsuRepository.commandExecutor.gitRemoteList()` — already implemented
- `JujutsuRevisionNumber` wraps a `Revision` (ChangeId), but NOT CommitId — need to expose CommitId somehow

### Key Decision: How to Get CommitId in File History

`JujutsuFileRevision` has access to `LogEntry` (which has `commitId`), but the platform action only sees `VcsFileRevision`. Options:
1. Add a `commitId` property to `JujutsuFileRevision` (simplest — cast in action)
2. Store both ChangeId and CommitId in `JujutsuRevisionNumber`
3. Pass LogEntry reference through

Option 1 is simplest and follows the pattern Git uses (casting to GitFileRevision).

### Scope

- GitHub and GitLab support (covers most jj users)
- Commit view only initially (file view is nice-to-have)
- Opens in default browser via `BrowserUtil.browse()`
- Graceful handling: disabled when no recognized remote, no error dialogs

### Test Plan

- Unit test `RemoteUrlBuilder` with GitHub SSH, GitHub HTTPS, GitLab SSH, GitLab HTTPS, unknown hosts
- Unit test remote list parsing
- Manual: runIde with a repo that has GitHub/GitLab remotes, verify URLs open correctly from log and file history
close_reason: Implemented RemoteUrlBuilder (github.com/gitlab.com SSH+HTTPS parsing), openInRemoteGroup (dynamic per-remote actions with single/submenu behavior), OpenInRemoteFromHistoryGroup for file history toolbar. Unit tests pass.

title: Display bookmarks as tags in log view commit list
description:
**Problem:**
The log view doesn't visually display bookmarks next to commits like the Git plugin shows branches. Bookmarks are Jujutsu's equivalent to Git branches and should be prominently visible in the log.

**Current Behavior:**
- Log view shows commit graph and descriptions
- No visual indication of which commits have bookmarks
- Users can't quickly identify bookmarked commits

**Expected Behavior:**
- Show bookmark names as colored tags/labels next to commit descriptions (like Git branches)
- Display bookmark icon/indicator
- Use distinct colors for different bookmarks (similar to Git's branch coloring)
- Show multiple bookmarks if a commit has several
- Distinguish between local and remote bookmarks

**Example (Git behavior):**
```
* abc1234 (main, origin/main) Commit message
* def5678 (feature-branch) Another commit
```

**Implementation Notes:**
- JujutsuLogProvider.readAllRefsInternal() already reads bookmarks
- Bookmarks are returned as VcsRef with type BOOKMARK
- Need to ensure refs are properly rendered in the log UI
- May need to configure VcsLogRefManager.BOOKMARK type for proper display

**Related:**
- Issue #18: Bookmark management UI (mentions showing bookmarks but not specifically about visual display)
- Issue #5: Bookmark management system

**Priority:** HIGH - Bookmarks are essential for navigation in Jujutsu workflows

title: File history view (VcsHistoryProvider)
description:
**Priority**: HIGH (Phase 1 - Read-only features)
**Effort**: 2-3 days

## Description
Implement VcsHistoryProvider to show per-file revision history.

## User Experience
- Right-click file → "Show History"
- View all changes that touched the file
- See change IDs, descriptions, dates
- Compare any two revisions

## Implementation
```kotlin
class JujutsuHistoryProvider(private val vcs: JujutsuVcs) : VcsHistoryProvider {
    override fun createSessionFor(filePath: FilePath): VcsHistorySession? {
        // Execute: jj log path/to/file
        // Parse into List<VcsFileRevision>
        // Return JujutsuHistorySession
    }
}
```

## Commands
- `jj log --template=<fields> path/to/file`
- `jj file show -r <change-id> path/to/file`

## Registration
Add to `JujutsuVcs.kt`:
```kotlin
override fun getVcsHistoryProvider() = JujutsuHistoryProvider(this)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Right-click "Show History" works
- [ ] Shows all revisions for file
- [ ] Change IDs, descriptions, dates displayed
- [ ] Can compare any two revisions
- [ ] Threading: runs on background thread
- [ ] Integrates with IntelliJ history UI

## References
See ROADMAP.md Phase 1 - Read-only Features
See VCS_API_REFERENCE.md - VcsHistoryProvider section

title: Real-time status updates (auto-refresh)
description:
**Priority**: MEDIUM (Phase 1 - Read-only features)
**Effort**: 1-2 days

## Description
Auto-refresh working copy status when files change on disk.

## User Experience
- File changes appear immediately in tool window
- Status colors update automatically
- No manual refresh needed

## Implementation
```kotlin
class JujutsuFileListener(private val project: Project) : VirtualFileListener {
    override fun contentsChanged(event: VirtualFileEvent) {
        VcsDirtyScopeManager.getInstance(project).fileDirty(event.file)
    }
}
```

## Registration
In `JujutsuVcs.activate()`:
```kotlin
override fun activate() {
    super.activate()
    VirtualFileManager.getInstance().addVirtualFileListener(fileListener, this)
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File edits trigger automatic refresh
- [ ] File creation triggers refresh
- [ ] File deletion triggers refresh
- [ ] Listener registered in activate()
- [ ] No performance issues with large projects
- [ ] Optional: debouncing for rapid changes

## References
See ROADMAP.md Phase 1 - Read-only Features
See VCS_API_REFERENCE.md - Virtual File Listener

title: Cmd+D in working copy defaults to editor action
description: With the working copy window open, hitting Cmd+D actually defaults to the editor action, which is typically to duplicate the current line. This differs from behaviour when the project window is open - where the project window action takes precedence. This has been reported here: https://github.com/kkkev/jj-idea/issues/12\#issuecomment-4377534649
close_reason: Closed

title: RestoreToChange not available in historical file editors
description: Why: Restoring a file to a historical revision should be available when viewing a historical version in an editor, but RestoreToChangeAction is only in fileChangeActionGroup() (changes tree context menu) and not in Jujutsu.EditorGroup. In editor context, e.logEntry (LOG_ENTRY DataSink key) is null because it's not injected — only VIRTUAL_FILE_LOG_ENTRY user data on the VirtualFile is set. Fix: 1) Add Jujutsu.RestoreToChange to Jujutsu.EditorGroup in plugin.xml. 2) Make RestoreToChangeAction.update() also resolve logEntry via possibleLogEntryFor(e.file) as a fallback when LOG_ENTRY is null. 3) In actionPerformed(), use the same fallback.
close_reason: Closed

title: Open in Remote for historical editor uses pinned commit ID without checking if it's pushed
description: Why: manual-tests.md line 218 expects Open in Remote to work for historical editors viewing unpushed commits by resolving the latest pushed ancestor. OpenInRemoteFromEditorGroup.actionPerformed() uses pinnedCommitId (from VIRTUAL_FILE_LOG_ENTRY) directly for the URL when the commit is not the working copy. If the commit is unpushed (immutable==false), the URL will point to a non-existent commit. Fix: in openInRemote(), when pinnedCommitId is not null, check if it is immutable — if not immutable, call latestPushedAncestorCommitId(remote.name, revision=pinnedCommitId) instead of using it directly. This matches how OpenFileInRemoteGroup handles the same situation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Open in Remote hidden in working copy panel
description: Why: manual-tests.md line 155 expects 'Open in Remote' to be available in the working copy panel context menu. OpenFileInRemoteGroup.update() explicitly hides when entry.isWorkingCopy==true (line 43). The working copy panel does not inject LOG_ENTRY, so singleRepoForFiles.workingCopy is used — but that entry has isWorkingCopy=true, causing the action to hide. Fix: allow working copy context and resolve via latestPushedAncestorCommitId() to get a valid commit hash for the URL. Needs to handle case where no pushed ancestor exists (show notification).
notes:
OpenFileInRemoteGroup.update() explicitly checks entry.isWorkingCopy at line 43 and hides. In working copy panel context, e.logEntry is null (no DataSink LOG_ENTRY), so the group falls back via singleRepoForFiles.workingCopy — that entry has isWorkingCopy=true, triggering the hide.
Fix: remove the isWorkingCopy early-exit; instead call latestPushedAncestorCommitId() to resolve a valid commit hash for the URL. If no pushed ancestor exists, show a balloon notification rather than hiding the action silently. The action already calls hasPushedAncestor() so that infrastructure exists.
close_reason: Closed

title: CompareWithBranch invisible in changes tree context (details panel and WC panel)
description: Why: manual-tests.md lines 63 and 151 expect 'Compare with Another Commit' in the context menu of the changes tree in the log details panel and working copy panel. CompareFileWithBranchAction.update() checks e.repoForFile which requires VIRTUAL_FILE to be in the DataContext. In changes tree context, only CHANGES (VcsDataKeys.CHANGES) are available, so VIRTUAL_FILE is null, so repoForFile is null, so the action is hidden. Fix: update() should also check e.singleRepoForFiles (which resolves from file paths in the changes) as a fallback.
close_reason: Closed

title: CompareWithLocal and CompareBeforeWithLocal invisible in historical file editors
description: Why: manual-tests.md line 211 expects 'Compare with local' to be visible in the Jujutsu menu for historical editors. Both CompareWithLocalAction and CompareBeforeWithLocalAction extend HistoricalVersionAction whose update() reads e.logEntry (JujutsuDataKeys.LOG_ENTRY, the DataSink key). In editor context, LOG_ENTRY is never injected into the DataContext — only VIRTUAL_FILE_LOG_ENTRY user data is set on the VirtualFile. So both actions are invisible. Fix: HistoricalVersionAction.update() should also check possibleLogEntryFor(e.file) as a fallback when LOG_ENTRY is null.
close_reason: Closed

title: CompareBeforeWithAnotherCommit: new action to compare parent version with chosen revision
description: Why: Five places in the manual tests expect a 'Compare Before with Another Commit' action (details panel, working copy panel, project tool window, current file editors, historical editors). Currently only 'Compare Before with Local' exists. Need a new action analogous to CompareBeforeWithLocalAction but using RevisionSelectorPopup (like CompareFileWithBranchAction) to let user pick the right-hand revision instead of always comparing against local. What to do: 1) Create CompareBeforeWithBranchAction that shows parent's file version on LHS and a user-chosen revision on RHS. 2) Register it in plugin.xml. 3) Add to fileChangeActionGroup() and Jujutsu.EditorGroup. 4) Update manual-tests.md to check it off.
notes:
Mirror CompareBeforeWithLocalAction but use RevisionSelectorPopup for the chosen revision instead of always comparing against local. Key implementation points:
- Use logEntryForFile (not logEntry) so it works in both changes tree and editor contexts
- In changes tree: LHS = change.before (parent content), RHS = file at chosen revision
- In editor context (changes empty): LHS = f.filePath.fileAt(logEntry.parentContentLocator), RHS = f.filePath.fileAt(chosenRevision)
- Register in both fileChangeActionGroup() and Jujutsu.EditorGroup (alongside CompareBeforeWithLocal)
- Use RevisionSelectorPopup.Filter(true, true) same as CompareFileWithBranchAction

title: Open Local File
description: Add a menu item to open a local file when a historically changed file is selected. This should open the file from the working copy, if it is still there.
notes: In editor context, e.file is a JujutsuVirtualFile with a historical contentLocator. The local working copy version is at e.file.filePath.virtualFile (the regular on-disk VirtualFile at that path). Action should be visible only when that local file still exists (virtualFile != null). Use OpenFileDescriptor(project, localFile) to navigate to it. Works naturally in Jujutsu.EditorGroup since e.file is available there.

title: Allow restore of multiple files
description:
There are two restore actions that work in different contexts, and both have faults.

RestoreToChangeAction only works on a single change.

RestoreSelectionAction only works if all selected files share a single repository. Make this work for sets of files across repositories. While there, look for other uses of singleRepoForFiles and refactor accordingly.

title: No Jujutsu submenu when viewing a historical file in editor
description: When opening a historical version of a file (e.g., via Show History or from annotation gutter), the editor context menu has no Jujutsu submenu / annotate option. The Jujutsu actions are only available when editing the working copy file.
notes:
Status as of 2026-05-12:
FIXED: Show Diff from historical editor now diffs the historical version against its parent (not the working copy)
FIXED: Compare with Another Commit from historical editor uses the historical version (not working copy)

STILL OPEN:

title: click on annotation should open change in log
description: When a user clicks on the change id column in a file annotation, it should open that change in the log. Ideally, the change id would be presented as a link. Maybe this should be done after refactoring annotations, so that we can draw on the existing change id rendering?

The bug is likely that the notification doesn't produce a visible result (log tab not open or not responding to changeSelection). Debug handleAnnotationClick. Also: add ColorKey for change ID column so it renders in the plugin's change ID color, and set ChangeIdAspect showByDefault=true.

title: describe previous revision has placeholder in title

title: log window contains old history integration

This is slightly unexpected behaviour - research whether it should be here at all. If it is not possible to delete a remote bookmark, then there should be no action for it in the submenu. However... it should be possible to remove a bookmark, then push that removal to delete it from the remote. Research options around this.

title: Refresh plugin metadata and roadmap for recently released features
description: Update plugin.xml key features + roadmap paragraph, and trim ROADMAP.md to reflect shipped Open in Remote, file-menu squash/split, and track/untrack bookmarks (0.5.11 through 0.6.3).

title: diff from working copy fails sometimes from merge
description: If the working copy is a merge, then the diff tries to compare to @-, but this is ambiguous for a merge operation. Instead, it fails - sometimes saying that the diff cannot be loaded, and other times just showing the new file as completely new, with no old version.
close_reason: Fixed by resolving @- to first parent change id via cached working copy state. Approach B (reverse-apply jj diff --git for correct auto-merged content) tracked in jj-idea-ayk7 as P3 follow-up.

title: Diff views: include Open In > Remote in editor context menus
description: Show Diff, Compare with Local, Compare Before with Local, Compare with Another Commit, and the working-copy Show Diff actions all open IntelliJ DiffManager diff viewers. The editors inside these diff viewers need Open In > Remote actions available, ideally by using the same full read-only editor context menus (including navigation, history etc.) that appear in regular editors. This is a prerequisite for adding line-number support when opening diff-view files in remote.
notes: All diff actions (ShowChangesDiffAction, CompareWithLocalAction, CompareBeforeWithLocalAction, CompareFileWithBranchAction) use standard IntelliJ DiffManager.showDiff(). Need to investigate how to inject Open In > Remote into diff editor context menus — either via DiffEditorPopupActionGroup, a DiffExtension, or by ensuring diff viewers use editor contexts that already include the RevealGroup.
close_reason: Added DiffContentInfo to JujutsuDataKeys; annotate historical DiffContent in ShowChangesDiffAction, CompareWithLocalAction, CompareBeforeWithLocalAction; extend OpenInRemoteFromEditorGroup with repoForFileOrDiff/diffContentInfo fallback; registered in Diff.EditorPopupMenu. CompareFileWithBranchAction historical side deferred to jj-idea-345n.

title: Open Repository Version: open proper editor with RevealGroup/Open In menus
description: Currently OpenRepositoryVersionAction opens files via ContentRevisionVirtualFile in a standard editor, but the editor's Open In > Remote menu doesn't work because possibleJujutsuRepositoryFor() doesn't recognise ContentRevisionVirtualFile as belonging to a JJ repo. Fix this so that when a historical version is opened, the editor has working Open In > Remote actions (and ideally the full standard read-only editor navigation menus). This is a prerequisite for adding line-number support when opening history entries in remote.
notes: OpenRepositoryVersionAction opens files as ContentRevisionVirtualFile. The Open In > Remote action (OpenInRemoteFromEditorGroup in RevealGroup) fails because possibleJujutsuRepositoryFor() can't resolve a ContentRevisionVirtualFile to a repo. Fix: either make the repo resolver recognise ContentRevisionVirtualFile, or register a separate Open In > Remote action that can pull the revision and repo context from the virtual file's ContentRevision.
close_reason: Closed

title: open in remote at line
description: When opening a file in remote from the editor, respect the line number or selection
design:
## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1 — Editor (independent)

**Line mapping approach**: Capture local document text + caret/selection on EDT before runInBackground. In background: fetch remote file content via commandExecutor.show(commitHash, relativePath). Use ComparisonManager.getInstance().compareLines(remoteText, localText, DEFAULT, EmptyProgressIndicator()) to get List<LineFragment>. Walk fragments: lines in context sections get remoteLine = localLine + accumulatedDelta; lines inside a locally-changed block map to the start of that block's remote range. Fallback: if remote fetch fails, open without fragment.

**URL fragment format**:
- GitHub: single=#L42, range=#L42-L50
- GitLab: single=#L42, range=#L42-50

**Files to change**:
1. RemoteUrlBuilder.kt — add lineRange: IntRange? = null to fileUrl(); append fragment based on kind
2. OpenInRemoteFromEditorGroup.kt — capture localText + localRange on EDT, call mapToRemoteRange() in background, pass result to fileUrl()
3. RemoteUrlBuilderTest.kt — tests for single line / range fragments for both GitHub and GitLab

### Phase 2 — History/Diff views (blocked by dependencies)
Once jj-idea-7e5n and jj-idea-6dvh are done, extend to pass line numbers when opening from diff viewers and Open Repository Version editors (caret available from the diff viewer's editor component).
notes:
Prerequisites: jj-idea-7e5n (Open Repository Version editor menus) and jj-idea-6dvh (Diff view editor menus) must be done before Phase 2 (history/diff case). Phase 1 (editor case) can be done independently.

Line number mapping approach: fetch remote file content via commandExecutor.show(), use IntelliJ ComparisonManager.compareLines() to build a line map, then walk fragments to translate local line numbers to remote. Lines inside locally-added blocks map to the start of that block's remote range.
close_reason: Closed

title: Open commit in remote
description: During development of open-in-remote, there was momentarily a menu item to open a commit from the log in a remote. This has now disappeared. This task is to bring that back, for commits that are present on the remote. Presence on the remote could be determined from existence of a remote bookmark at or ahead of the commit.
close_reason: Implemented via OpenInRemoteFromHistoryGroup and openInRemoteGroup in log context menu respectively. Covered by refactoring in this change.

title: Open in remote for historical version editor
description: When in an editor for a file at a particular version in the history, there is no Open in > remote menu item to open that version of that file in e.g. Github.
close_reason: Implemented via OpenInRemoteFromHistoryGroup and openInRemoteGroup in log context menu respectively. Covered by refactoring in this change.

title: Disable push/fetch when no Git remote configured
description: When no Git remote has been set up, push and fetch actions currently allow the user to attempt the operation only to fail with 'No git remote named origin'. These actions should be disabled (grayed out) when no remotes exist. Remote management UI is not yet implemented, so no cue is provided yet.
close_reason: Closed

title: Filter log by repo
description: Add a 'repository' filter to the log window for projects with multiple initialised repositories.
close_reason: Fixed scoping bug: add() was called on UnifiedJujutsuLogPanel (this) instead of the filterPanel parameter

title: Push always picks one repo
description: The push action only ever works on the first initialised repository that it encounters in the list. This is broken. We need to determine the repository from the action, which means that the push action always needs to be invoked from the scope of a file, not globally.

title: Show meaningful error and config popup for invalid jj executable
description: GitHub #3: When jj is not in PATH (e.g. in ~/bin/ not on system PATH), plugin just shows generic errors in internal errors dialog. Should show a meaningful error message and offer to configure the jj executable path. Related to jj-idea-4eq (closed) and jj-idea-3ap. See https://github.com/kkkev/jj-idea/issues/3
close_reason: https://github.com/kkkev/jj-idea/issues/3 is now closed - problem fixed in previous revision

title: Changed jj executable path not effective until IDE restart
description: GitHub #4: After changing the jj executable path in settings, the new path is not used. Old errors persist. Switching VCS mapping away and back also uses old path. Only IDE restart picks up the change. See https://github.com/kkkev/jj-idea/issues/4
close_reason: Fixed two bugs: (1) connect() replay now calls handler directly instead of broadcasting to all subscribers, (2) apply() uses direct state invalidation instead of fragile one-shot subscription. Also added recheck on Test button for upgraded-binary scenario.

title: Improve repository label naming in multi-repo mode
description: GitHub #10: Repo labels are inconsistent - main project should use simple name, others shown by path. Should use simple names when unique, add parent directory only when ambiguous. See https://github.com/kkkev/jj-idea/issues/10
close_reason: Fixed by moving repository creation to a single place where all display names can be determined upfront, differentiating duplicates where necessary.

title: Show combined diff when selecting multiple changes in log
description: GitHub #11: When multiple changes are selected in the log, show a combined diff (like Git log does). Currently details get empty. Should work for arbitrary non-contiguous commits. Related to jj-idea-c9d. See https://github.com/kkkev/jj-idea/issues/11
close_reason: Implemented as part of jj-idea-c9d work. Union/zip of changes shown in the tree, each file's diff spans oldest-to-newest revision across all selected commits.

title: Warn about sideways pushes
description: In some remote repositories, it is important not to 'force-push' a branch. In jj terms, this means not pushing bookmarks that would move them sideways. By default, the push action should prevent sideways moves, but give the option to opt in to allow it.
close_reason: Closed

title: Settings changes require IDE restart to take effect
description:
## Problem
When users change the jj executable path in Settings → Version Control → Jujutsu, the change doesn't take effect until the IDE is restarted.

## Expected behavior
Settings changes should take effect immediately, triggering:
- Re-validation of jj executable
- Refresh of VCS state
- Retry of any pending operations

## Related
- GitHub issue #4: Changed `jj` executable only effective after IDE restart

## Implementation notes
Settings service should fire application message bus event when settings change. VCS and other components should listen and invalidate cached state.
close_reason: Implemented provider pattern: CliExecutor now takes () -> String instead of String, reading fresh settings on each command execution
