title: Add split operation
description:
Allow users to split a change into two changes. This is a core JJ operation for breaking up large changes into smaller, focused commits.

The CLI layer (CommandExecutor.split + CliExecutor.splitArgs) and tests (CliExecutorSplitTest) are already implemented. This issue covers the UI/action wiring: the SplitDialog, the splitAction factory, context menu integration, and bundle properties.

## What's Already Done
- CommandExecutor.split() interface method
- CliExecutor.splitArgs() implementation
- CliExecutorSplitTest (9 test cases)
- FileSelectionPanel (shared with squash)

## What's Remaining
- SplitDialog (ui/split/SplitDialog.kt) — new file
- splitAction factory (actions/change/splitAction.kt) — new file
- Context menu entry in JujutsuLogContextMenuActions
- Bundle properties (~13 keys)
- Dialog tests (SplitDialogTest)
- Context menu availability tests

## Related
- jj-idea-1ra: Earlier split design (this issue supersedes it with more detail)
- jj-idea-u2ct: File-level entry points for squash/split (follow-up, not in scope here)
- jj-idea-glx1: Squash operation (completed — split mirrors this pattern)
design:
# Split Operation — Detailed Design

## Overview

A **"Split..."** action in the log context menu opens a dialog letting the user choose which files stay in the original commit and which move to a new child (or sibling) commit. Follows the same pattern as squash: action factory -> dialog -> command execution -> refresh.

## Scope

**In scope:**
- Split a single mutable change via `jj split -r <rev>`
- File-level selection (checked = stays, unchecked = moves to new child)
- Description editing for the first commit
- Parallel commits option (`--parallel`)

**Out of scope:**
- Interactive hunk-level selection (`--interactive`) — future enhancement
- Custom description for the second commit — jj uses the original description or prompts
- File-level entry points (editors, working copy panel, details panel) — see jj-idea-u2ct

## Entry Point: Log Context Menu

Add **"Split..."** to `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.createActionGroup()`, in the same separator group as Rebase and Squash (after the squash action, line 65 area).

### Availability Rules
Same pattern as abandon (simpler than squash — no parent mutability check needed):
- Single selection only (`entries.singleOrNull()`)
- Mutable changes only (`!entry.immutable`)
- Works on working copy too (split @)
- Works on merge commits (all files still splittable)

```kotlin
// In JujutsuLogContextMenuActions:
add(splitAction(project, entry?.takeIf { !it.immutable }))
```

No `splitableEntry()` helper needed (unlike squash which checks parent mutability). The `nullAndDumbAwareAction` disables the action when entry is null.

## Dialog Design: SplitDialog

A `DialogWrapper` modeled on `SquashDialog`, with vertical layout. Located at `ui/split/SplitDialog.kt`.

### Layout (top to bottom)

```
+-----------------------------------------------------+
| Split Change                                    [X]  |
+------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                      |
| Source                                               |
| +--------------------------------------------------+ |
| | * ksqwmnop  Add auth and logging    main @       | |
| +--------------------------------------------------+ |
|                                                      |
| Files to keep in first commit                        |
| +-toolbar: Expand All | Collapse All | Group By --+ |
| | [x] src/main/Auth.kt        (Added)             | |
| | [x] src/main/AuthService.kt (Added)             | |
| | [ ] src/main/Logger.kt      (Added)             | |
| | [ ] src/main/LogConfig.kt   (Added)             | |
| +--------------------------------------------------+ |
|                                                      |
| First commit: 2 files - Second commit: 2 files      |
|                                                      |
| Description for first commit                         |
| +--------------------------------------------------+ |
| | Add auth and logging                             | |
| |                                                  | |
| +--------------------------------------------------+ |
|                                                      |
| [ ] Create parallel commits                         |
|                                                      |
|                          [Split] [Cancel]            |
+------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Section Details

#### 1. Source Panel (read-only, fixed height)
HTML-rendered change info, same pattern as `SquashDialog.createEntryPane()`:
- Status indicators (working copy marker, immutable indicator)
- Change ID (colored) + description + decorations (bookmarks)
- Uses `IconAwareHtmlPane` + `htmlString { }` from `ui/components/`

#### 2. File Selection Panel (expandable, main area)
Uses the shared `FileSelectionPanel` from `ui/common/FileSelectionPanel.kt`.

**Checkbox semantics:**
- **Checked** = file stays in the original (first) commit
- **Unchecked** = file moves to the new child (second) commit
- All files checked by default — user unchecks files to move them out

This matches `jj split`'s CLI semantics where filesets specify what STAYS.

**File loading:** Call `ChangeService.loadChanges(entry)` on a background thread (same two-phase async pattern as squash), then pass to `FileSelectionPanel.setChanges()`.

#### 3. Summary Line (dynamic, between files and description)
A `JBLabel` that updates whenever checkboxes change:
- "First commit: N files - Second commit: M files"
- Updates via a checkbox change listener on the FileSelectionPanel's tree

This serves as a lightweight preview — no need for a separate preview panel since the checkbox tree itself shows the split visually.

#### 4. Description Panel (editable, fixed height ~4 lines)
A `JBTextArea` for the first commit's description.

**Pre-population:** The original description of the source commit (unlike squash which merges parent+source descriptions).

**Rationale for no second description field:** `jj split` handles the second commit's description automatically — it receives the original description. Adding a second text area would complicate the UI for a rare need. Users can `jj describe` the second commit afterwards.

#### 5. Options (single row)
- **"Create parallel commits"** checkbox (unchecked by default) -> maps to `--parallel` flag
- Tooltip: "Create sibling commits instead of parent/child"

### Validation
- At least one file must be unchecked (otherwise no split happens — show validation error on FileSelectionPanel)
- At least one file must be checked (otherwise original becomes empty — allow but it's fine, jj handles this)

### Data Structure

```kotlin
data class SplitSpec(
    val revision: Revision,
    val filePaths: List<FilePath>,   // Files for first commit (checked). Empty = all (no split).
    val description: Description?,   // -m flag for first commit. null = no override.
    val parallel: Boolean            // --parallel flag
)
```

### Dialog Result Logic (doOKAction)

```kotlin
// If all files are checked, filePaths is empty (means "all" to jj, but validation prevents this)
// If some files are checked, filePaths = list of checked files
val filePaths = if (fileSelection.allIncluded) {
    emptyList()
} else {
    fileSelection.includedChanges.mapNotNull { it.filePath }
}

// Only pass description if user changed it from the original
val description = descriptionField.text.trim().let { text ->
    if (text == sourceEntry.description.actual) null
    else Description(text)
}
```

## Action Factory: splitAction.kt

Located at `actions/change/splitAction.kt`. Follows the squash action pattern exactly.

```kotlin
fun splitAction(project: Project, entry: LogEntry?) =
    nullAndDumbAwareAction(entry, "log.action.split", AllIcons.Actions.SplitVertically) {
        ApplicationManager.getApplication().executeOnPooledThread {
            val changes = ChangeService.loadChanges(target)

            ApplicationManager.getApplication().invokeLater {
                val dialog = SplitDialog(project, target, changes)
                if (!dialog.showAndGet()) return@invokeLater

                val spec = dialog.result ?: return@invokeLater
                target.repo.commandExecutor
                    .createCommand { split(spec.revision, spec.filePaths, spec.description, spec.parallel) }
                    .onSuccess {
                        target.repo.invalidate(select = target.id)
                        log.info("Split ${'$'}{target.id}")
                    }
                    .onFailure { tellUser(project, "log.action.split.error") }
                    .executeAsync()
            }
        }
    }
```

### Post-Split Behavior
- Select the **original change** (`target.id`) after split — it retains its change ID with the kept files
- The new child/sibling commit gets a new change ID (visible in the refreshed log)
- No compound operations needed (unlike squash which may do `edit @-` after)

## Context Menu Integration

In `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.createActionGroup()`, add after the squash action:

```kotlin
add(squashAction(project, squashableEntry(entry, allEntries), allEntries))
add(splitAction(project, entry?.takeIf { !it.immutable }))
```

## Icon

Use `AllIcons.Actions.SplitVertically` — built-in IntelliJ icon that visually represents splitting. If a custom icon is created later (jj-idea-9629), it can be swapped in.

## Bundle Properties

Add to `JujutsuBundle.properties`:

```properties
log.action.split=Split...
log.action.split.tooltip=Split this change into two changes
log.action.split.error.title=Split Failed
log.action.split.error.message=Failed to split change:\n{0}
dialog.split.title=Split Change
dialog.split.source=Source
dialog.split.files=Files to keep in first commit
dialog.split.files.hint=Checked files stay in the original commit; unchecked files move to a new commit
dialog.split.description=Description for first commit
dialog.split.parallel=Create parallel commits
dialog.split.parallel.tooltip=Create sibling commits instead of parent/child
dialog.split.no.unchecked=Uncheck at least one file to split
dialog.split.button=Split
dialog.split.summary=First commit: {0} files \u00B7 Second commit: {1} files
```

## File Structure

**New files:**
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/actions/change/splitAction.kt` — Action factory
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/split/SplitDialog.kt` — Dialog

**Modified files:**
- `ui/log/JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt` — Add split to context menu + import
- `messages/JujutsuBundle.properties` — Add message keys

**Already done (no changes needed):**
- `jj/CommandExecutor.kt` — `split()` method already exists
- `jj/cli/CliExecutor.kt` — `splitArgs()` already implemented
- `test/.../CliExecutorSplitTest.kt` — 9 CLI arg tests already passing

## Tests

### Context Menu Availability
Add to `JujutsuLogContextMenuActionsTest.kt`:
- Mutable entry -> split action enabled
- Immutable entry -> split action disabled
- Working copy -> split action enabled
- Multi-select -> split action disabled (only single selection)

### Dialog Tests (SplitDialogTest.kt)
- All files checked by default
- Summary line updates on checkbox change
- Unchecking file -> appears in filePaths
- All checked -> validation error
- Description pre-populated with source description
- Parallel checkbox default off
- Result contains correct SplitSpec

## Design Rationale

**Checked = stays, unchecked = moves:** Matches `jj split`'s CLI semantics where filesets specify what stays. Users think "I want to keep these files here and move the rest out." This is also consistent with the squash dialog where checked = included in squash.

**No separate preview panel:** The checkbox tree IS the preview. The summary line ("First commit: 2 files - Second commit: 3 files") provides at-a-glance confirmation without duplicating information. A full two-pane preview would add complexity without proportional value.

**No second description field:** `jj split` passes the original description to the second commit automatically. Users who need a different description can `jj describe` afterwards. Adding a second text area would make the dialog more complex for a rare use case.

**Parallel option as simple checkbox:** It's a single flag with clear semantics. Most users leave it unchecked (parent/child is the default and most common). Power users who want sibling commits can toggle it easily.

**Select original after split:** The original change keeps its change ID but with fewer files. Selecting it lets the user immediately verify the result. The new child is visible in the log just below.
close_reason: Implemented split UI: SplitDialog, splitAction factory, context menu entry, bundle properties, and dialog tests. All tests and ktlint pass.

title: Race condition between editing and JJ commands
description:
1. Edit a file, don't save or give time to auto-save.
2. Perform a jj command that changes the file, e.g. jj edit.

This causes a memory/file conflict.

Resolve this by forcing a save of all open files before performing jj commands that could change the working copy.
close_reason: Added FileDocumentManager.saveAllDocuments() to both executeAsync() and executeWithProgress() in CommandExecutor.Command

title: (BUG) Squash into Parent does not select parent in log after operation
description:
After squashing the working copy into its parent (with 'keep emptied' unchecked), the log does not select the parent change (which is now the working copy after the squash+edit@- sequence).

## Root cause investigation so far
The squash action calls `invalidate(select = parentEntry.id)`. The pending selection mechanism in JujutsuLogTable was partially fixed: setEntries no longer overwrites explicit pending selections with auto-reselect. However, selection still fails.

Possible causes to investigate:
1. The ChangeKey uses the old repo object which may not match after refresh (object identity vs value equality)
2. The parentEntry.id may not match after squash because jj may rewrite the parent's commit hash (the change ID stays the same but CommitId changes) — need to verify ChangeId.equals handles this
3. The selection notify may fire before the file listener triggers data reload, and then get consumed by stale data
4. The debounced file listener refresh may overwrite the pending selection (setEntries is called multiple times)

## Steps to reproduce
1. Have a working copy with changes and a mutable parent
2. Right-click working copy in log → Squash into Parent
3. Click Squash (with 'keep emptied' unchecked)
4. Expected: parent (now working copy) is selected in log
5. Actual: no entry selected, or wrong entry selected

## Related
- jj-idea-q3lk: Refresh suppression for compound operations (may help with timing issues)
- jj-idea-i4k5: logRefresh notifier
close_reason: Fixed race condition: pendingSelection now only cleared on successful selectEntry()

This will also make it easier to validate versions of jj supportable by each version of the plugin and figure out where incompatibilities lie when jj is upgraded.

class FileSelectionPanel(project: Project) : JPanel(BorderLayout()) {

}

title: Add squash operation UI
description:
Allow users to squash changes. This is a core JJ operation for combining a change into its parent.

## Commands
- `jj squash` — squash working copy into parent
- `jj squash -r <change-id>` — squash specific change into its parent

## Requirements
- Action available from log context menu ("Squash into Parent")
- Refresh log after operation
- Error handling for conflicts

## Related
- jj-idea-ah6: Rebase/squash operations UI (squash was deferred from this)
design:
# Squash Operation UI Design

## Overview

One context menu action — **"Squash into Parent..."** — that opens a dialog supporting both whole-change and selective-file squash. No need for separate subtasks; it's one cohesive feature with a single dialog.

The dialog follows the pattern established by the rebase dialog: action factory → dialog → command execution → refresh.

## Scope

**In scope (this issue)**:
- Squash a single mutable change into its parent: `jj squash -r <rev>`
- Squash selected files from a change into its parent: `jj squash -r <rev> <paths...>`
- Description editing for the combined result
- Option to keep the emptied source change

**Out of scope (future issues)**:
- `--from/--into` arbitrary source/destination squash (not just into parent)
- Multi-source squash (`--from A --from B --into C`)
- Interactive hunk-level selection (`--interactive`)

## Action Initiation

### Context Menu Entry
Add **"Squash into Parent..."** to the log table context menu, in the same separator group as Rebase (line 49 area of `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt`).

**Availability rules** (same pattern as abandon/edit):
- Single selection only (no multi-select — `jj squash` operates on one revision)
- Mutable changes only (`!entry.immutable`)
- Enabled for working copy too (squash @ into @-)

**Null-guard pattern**:
```kotlin
add(squashAction(project, entry?.takeIf { !it.immutable }))
```

## Dialog Design: SquashDialog

A `DialogWrapper` with vertical layout, simpler than RebaseDialog (no destination picker needed since destination is always the parent).

### Layout (top to bottom)

#### 1. Source Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the change being squashed — HTML-rendered like the rebase source panel:
- Change ID (colored) + description + decorations (bookmarks, working copy indicator)
- Author + timestamp

#### 2. Destination Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the parent change (destination of squash) — same HTML rendering:
- "Into parent:" label
- Change ID + description + decorations

#### 3. Files Panel (expandable, main area)
Uses the shared `FileSelectionPanel` from jj-idea-7aha.

**File loading**: Call `ChangeService.loadChanges(entry)` to get the file list for the source change, then pass to `FileSelectionPanel.setChanges()`. This handles rename detection and all status types.

**Checkbox behavior** (provided by IntelliJ's `ChangesTree` infrastructure via `FileSelectionPanel`):
- All files checked by default (= whole squash)
- User unchecks files to exclude them (= selective squash)
- Directory nodes show three-state: all/some/none children selected
- Click on directory checkbox toggles all children
- Space key toggles selected node(s)

#### 4. Description Panel (editable, fixed height ~4 lines)
A multiline text area for the squashed result's description.

**Pre-population strategy**: Concatenate source and destination descriptions, separated by a blank line (matching `jj squash` default behavior):
```
<destination description>

<source description>
```

If the source description is empty (`(no description set)`), just show the destination description.

#### 5. Options (single row)
- **"Keep emptied source"** checkbox (unchecked by default) → maps to `--keep-emptied` flag

### Dialog Buttons
- **"Squash"** button (OK action) — executes the squash
- **Cancel** button

### Validation
- At least one file must be selected (disable "Squash" button otherwise)
- If all files are selected, omit file paths from command (simpler, whole squash)

### Data Structure
```kotlin
data class SquashSpec(
    val revision: Revision,        // Source change to squash
    val filePaths: List<FilePath>,  // Empty = all files (whole squash)
    val description: Description?,  // null = let jj merge descriptions; non-null = -m flag
    val keepEmptied: Boolean        // --keep-emptied flag
)
```

## Command Executor

### Interface addition (`CommandExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
fun squash(
    revision: Revision = WorkingCopy,
    filePaths: List<FilePath> = emptyList(),
    description: Description? = null,
    keepEmptied: Boolean = false
): CommandResult
```

### CLI implementation (`CliExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
override fun squash(revision, filePaths, description, keepEmptied) =
    execute(root, squashArgs(revision, filePaths, description, keepEmptied))

internal fun squashArgs(
    revision: Revision,
    filePaths: List<FilePath>,
    description: Description?,
    keepEmptied: Boolean
): List<String> = buildList {
    add("squash")
    add("-r"); add(revision.toString())
    if (description != null) { add("-m"); add(description.actual) }
    if (keepEmptied) add("--keep-emptied")
    filePaths.forEach { add(it.relativeTo(root)) }
}
```

## Action Factory (`squashAction.kt`)

```kotlin
fun squashAction(project: Project, entry: LogEntry?) =
    nullAndDumbAwareAction(entry, "log.action.squash", <squash-icon>) {
        val repo = target.repo
        val changes = ChangeService.loadChanges(target)
        val dialog = SquashDialog(project, target, changes)
        if (!dialog.showAndGet()) return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        val spec = dialog.result ?: return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        repo.commandExecutor
            .createCommand { squash(spec.revision, spec.filePaths, spec.description, spec.keepEmptied) }
            .onSuccess { repo.invalidate(select = target.id) }
            .onFailureTellUser("log.action.squash.error", project, log)
            .executeAsync()
    }
```

## Refresh After Squash

Call `repo.invalidate(select = ...)`:
- If `keepEmptied = false`: select the parent (since source is gone) — but actually, the change ID persists since it's the destination. Select the destination (parent) change.
- If `keepEmptied = true`: select the source change (it's still there, just empty now)

## File Structure

New files:
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/vcs/actions/squashAction.kt` — Action factory
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/squash/SquashDialog.kt` — Dialog

Modified files:
- `CommandExecutor.kt` — Add `squash()` method
- `CliExecutor.kt` — Implement `squash()` + `squashArgs()`
- `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt` — Add squash to context menu
- `JujutsuBundle.properties` — Add message keys

## Bundle Properties
```properties
log.action.squash=Squash into Parent...
log.action.squash.tooltip=Squash this change into its parent
log.action.squash.error.title=Squash Failed
log.action.squash.error.message=Failed to squash:\n{0}
dialog.squash.title=Squash into Parent
dialog.squash.source=Source
dialog.squash.destination=Into parent
dialog.squash.files=Files to squash
dialog.squash.files.none=Select at least one file
dialog.squash.description=Combined description
dialog.squash.keep.emptied=Keep emptied source change
dialog.squash.button=Squash
```

## Tests

- `CliExecutorSquashTest.kt` — Verify CLI arg construction for all flag combinations (whole, selective, with description, keep-emptied)
- `JujutsuLogContextMenuActionsTest.kt` — Add squash availability tests (immutable filtered out, single-select only, mutable allowed)

## Design Rationale

**Single action with file tree vs. two separate actions**: A single dialog with checkboxes (all checked by default) covers both whole and selective squash. Users who want whole squash just click "Squash" immediately. Users who want selective squash uncheck files. This matches IntelliJ's commit dialog pattern and avoids polluting the context menu with two similar entries.

**No destination picker**: Unlike rebase, squash destination is always the parent. Showing a destination picker would add complexity for a rare use case (`--from/--into`). That can be a separate "Squash Into..." action in a future issue.

**Description pre-population**: Concatenating descriptions matches `jj squash` default behavior. Users can edit before confirming. This avoids the jarring experience of jj opening $EDITOR.

**Checkbox tree reuse**: The shared `FileSelectionPanel` (jj-idea-7aha) wraps `JujutsuChangesTree` with `showCheckboxes = true`. The three-state checkbox behavior, directory toggling, and keyboard handling come for free from the platform.

**ChangeService.loadChanges**: Uses the existing `ChangeService.loadChanges(entry)` method which handles rename detection and all status types, rather than rolling a custom `diffSummary` parser.

title: Log doesn't refresh after abandon
description: Abandoning a change other than the working copy does not result in the log refreshing - so the abandoned change remains on screen, and any further attempt to interact with it fails.
design:
## Root Cause

`abandonChangeAction` calls `repo.invalidate(select = WorkingCopy)`, which calls `repositoryStates.invalidate()`. The loader runs `jj log -r @` and compares with the previous value via `StateKey` equality. When abandoning a non-WC change, the working copy hasn't changed, so `NotifiableState` suppresses the notification. The log panel only subscribes to `repositoryStates`, so it never hears about the abandon → no refresh.

This is a symptom of the architectural mismatch described in jj-idea-i4k5: the log panel subscribes to a working-copy-state signal, but needs an unconditional "repo changed" signal.

## Fix

Implement jj-idea-i4k5 (add `logRefresh` notifier). The log panel subscribes to `logRefresh` instead of `repositoryStates`. Since `logRefresh` fires unconditionally from `repo.invalidate()`, the log always refreshes after abandon.

## Selection after abandon

Per comment: currently selects working copy even when abandoning a different change. After implementing this fix, the abandon action should select one of the abandoned change's parents instead of `WorkingCopy`. This requires the action to resolve parent IDs before abandoning (from the `LogEntry.parentIds`).
close_reason: Fixed: after abandoning a non-WC change, now selects the first parent instead of always selecting WorkingCopy. Log refresh was already fixed by jj-idea-i4k5 (logRefresh notifier).

title: Log sometimes goes backwards
description: The log has been observed to go backwards. Specifically, there are cases where a parent precedes its child. This confuses the log display. We need to ensure that the order in which Jujutsu returns the log follows the DAG of the changes.
notes:
## Investigation Results (Updated)

### Root Cause
The bug affects **ALL projects** (single and multi-root), not just multi-root.

**Location**: `UnifiedJujutsuLogDataLoader.kt:127`

```kotlin
allEntries = entriesByRepo.values.flatten()
    .sortedByDescending { it.authorTimestamp ?: it.committerTimestamp }
```

### Why It Affects All Projects

1. `JujutsuCustomLogTabManager` ALWAYS creates `UnifiedJujutsuLogPanel` (line 56)
2. `UnifiedJujutsuLogPanel` uses `UnifiedJujutsuLogDataLoader`
3. `UnifiedJujutsuLogDataLoader.loadCommits()` sorts ALL entries by timestamp
4. The single-root `JujutsuLogPanel` (which doesn't sort) exists but is **never used**

### Verified Example

In jj-idea project itself:
- `qp` (child, timestamp 14:07) has parent `yp` (timestamp 15:21)
- jj returns them correctly: `qp` before `yp` (children before parents)
- Plugin sorts by timestamp: `yp` (15:21) before `qp` (14:07)
- Graph displays incorrectly with parent before child

### Why Timestamp Sorting Breaks DAG

The graph layout algorithm (`LayoutCalculatorImpl`) assumes children come before parents:

```kotlin
// Line 97-98: Expects parent row > child row
val nonAdjacentParents = entry.parents.filter { parentId ->
    val parentRow = rowByChangeId[parentId]
    parentRow != null && parentRow > rowIndex + 1
}
```

When a parent appears first (due to timestamp sorting):
- Parent's lane is assigned without knowing about its children
- Child can't find parent at expected position
- Passthrough lines don't render correctly

### Proposed Fix

Remove the timestamp sorting entirely. JJ already returns commits in correct topological order. For multi-root projects, we need a proper merge algorithm that respects DAG constraints.

**Option 1 (Simple)**: For single-root, use jj's order directly. For multi-root, topologically sort after merging.

**Option 2 (Better UX)**: Stable topological sort that uses timestamp as tiebreaker only for unrelated commits (different branches/repos), while always respecting parent-child ordering.

title: Dynamically show/hide JJ and native log panes based on VCS roots
description:
Implement dynamic visibility of log panes based on the VCS roots configuration.

## Requirements

### Visibility Rules
- **All roots are JJ**: Hide native VCS log pane, show only custom JJ log
- **No roots are JJ**: Hide JJ log pane, show only native VCS log
- **Mixed roots (JJ + other VCS)**: Show both panes

### Dynamic Switching
Must handle roots changing at runtime:
- User adds/removes VCS mappings
- User initializes JJ in a directory
- User removes .jj directory

Panes should come back to life when roots change - avoid permanent disposal.

## Current Behavior
- ToolWindowEnabler suppresses native log tabs when JJ is detected
- JujutsuStartupActivity opens custom log on startup
- Both use listeners but may not handle all transitions cleanly

## Implementation Considerations
1. Use state model listener for root changes (already have `initializedRoots`)
2. Track pane visibility state separately from disposal
3. Consider lazy creation - don't create panes until needed
4. Handle the "suppressor" pattern more gracefully (currently removes content permanently)

## Related Issues
- jj-idea-8g6: Refactor JujutsuStartupActivity to state model listener
- jj-idea-9zmn: Refactor log window to show all roots with root filter

**Part of**: jj-idea-dhd (multi-root handling epic)

title: Refactor working copy tool window for multi-root support
description:
Refactor the working copy tool window to better support multi-root projects.

## Current Problem
The changes list currently shows all changes for all JJ roots, even though each pane theoretically shows only a single root.

## Proposed Design

### Single Pane Layout
Switch from multiple panes (one per root) to a single unified pane:

1. **Changes list at top** - Shows changes across all roots
2. **Working copy controls below** - Change ID, parent(s), description fields
   - Shows details for the selected change's root
   - If changes from multiple roots are selected: disable controls or show explanatory message

### Empty Root Navigation
Need a way to navigate to working copy controls for roots with no file changes.

**Options to consider:**
1. Always show root nodes in the changes tree (even when empty)
2. Separate root selector UI element (dropdown/tabs) for multi-root projects
3. Status bar or header showing current root with switcher

### Single-Root Behavior
For single-root projects, no root selection needed - working copy is unambiguous.

## Implementation Notes
- Changes tree already groups by directory; may need root-level grouping
- Working copy controls need to track "active root" based on selection
- Consider how this interacts with the log window (separate issue)

title: Aid jj installation and detection
description:
Help users who don't have jj installed or whose jj is not detected.

## Scenarios
1. **jj not installed** - Detect and offer guidance on installation
2. **jj not in PATH** - Help user locate or configure jj executable path
3. **jj version too old** - Block with clear explanation and upgrade guidance

## Version Compatibility Strategy
**Decision: Hard minimum version (jj 0.37.0+)**

**Rationale**:
- Some features (like `change_offset`) are critical for correct behavior
- Without `change_offset` (added 0.37.0), multiple commits can share a change ID, breaking UI assumptions
- Graceful degradation would add complexity and risk subtle bugs
- jj is actively developed; users can upgrade more easily than debugging weird plugin behavior

**Implementation**:
1. Check jj version on startup and when executable path changes
2. If version < minimum, show error notification with:
   - Clear explanation of why minimum version is required
   - Current version detected vs minimum required
   - Upgrade instructions based on detected environment

## Upgrade Assistance Features
**Auto-detect installation method and provide tailored guidance**:

1. **macOS + Homebrew**:
   - Detect if jj installed via brew (`brew list jj`)
   - Offer "Upgrade Now" button that runs `brew upgrade jj`
   - Show progress, handle errors

2. **Cargo**:
   - Detect if jj installed via cargo (check `~/.cargo/bin/jj`)
   - Provide command: `cargo install --force jujutsu`
   - Offer "Copy Command" button

3. **Other/Unknown**:
   - Link to jj installation docs
   - Show manual upgrade instructions

4. **After upgrade**:
   - Re-verify version
   - Trigger VCS refresh
   - Show success notification

## Related GitHub Issues
- #3: Invalid `jj` executable should give meaningful error or config popup
- #5: Document and verify supported jj version (specifically mentions `change_offset` in 0.37.0 vs 0.36.0)

## Related Beads
- jj-idea-4eq: Allow to initialize a jj root (P0)
- jj-idea-ebn6: Settings changes require IDE restart (P2)
close_reason: Implemented jj installation detection and user guidance. Added: JjVersion for version parsing, InstallMethod enum, JjExecutableFinder for multi-location search, PackageManagerDetector for install method detection, JjAvailabilityChecker project service, and notifications for unavailable/outdated/invalid jj installations.

title: Add split operation UI
description:
Allow users to split a change into multiple changes. This is a core JJ operation for breaking up large changes.

## Requirements
- Action available from log context menu
- UI to select which files/hunks go into each resulting change
- Refresh log after operation

## Related
- jj-idea-ah6: Rebase/squash operations UI
design:
# Split Operation UI Design

## Overview

One context menu action — **"Split..."** — that opens a dialog letting the user choose which files stay in the original commit and which move to a new child (or sibling) commit.

The dialog follows the pattern established by the rebase and squash dialogs: action factory → dialog → command execution → refresh.

## Scope

**In scope (this issue)**:
- Split a single mutable change: `jj split -r <rev>`
- Select files that stay in the first commit (checked = stays, unchecked = moves to new child)
- Description editing for the first commit
- Parallel commits option (`--parallel`)

**Out of scope (future issues)**:
- Interactive hunk-level selection (`--interactive`)
- Custom description for the second commit (jj prompts for this or uses remainder)

## CLI Reference

`jj split -r <rev> [-m <message>] [--parallel] [<filesets>...]`

- **Filesets** specify what STAYS in the first commit (original position)
- Remaining files move to a new child commit (or sibling with `--parallel`)
- If no filesets given, jj opens an interactive editor (we always pass filesets from the UI)

## Action Initiation

### Context Menu Entry
Add **"Split..."** to the log table context menu, in the same separator group as Rebase and Squash (line 49 area of `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt`).

**Availability rules** (same pattern as abandon/squash):
- Single selection only (no multi-select — `jj split` operates on one revision)
- Mutable changes only (`!entry.immutable`)
- Enabled for working copy too (split @)

**Null-guard pattern**:
```kotlin
add(splitAction(project, entry?.takeIf { !it.immutable }))
```

## Dialog Design: SplitDialog

A `DialogWrapper` with vertical layout.

### Layout (top to bottom)

#### 1. Source Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the change being split — HTML-rendered like the rebase source panel:
- Change ID (colored) + description + decorations (bookmarks, working copy indicator)
- Author + timestamp

Uses `IconAwareHtmlPane` + `htmlString {}` (same pattern as `RebaseDialog.createSourcePanel()`).

#### 2. File Selection Panel (expandable, main area)
Uses the shared `FileSelectionPanel` from jj-idea-7aha.

**Checkbox semantics**:
- **Checked** = file stays in the original (first) commit
- **Unchecked** = file moves to the new child commit
- All files checked by default (user unchecks files to move them out)

**File loading**: Call `ChangeService.loadChanges(entry)` to get the file list, then pass to `FileSelectionPanel.setChanges()`.

#### 3. Description Panel (editable, fixed height ~4 lines)
A multiline text area for the first commit's description.

**Pre-population**: Original description of the source commit.

#### 4. Options (single row)
- **"Create parallel commits"** checkbox (unchecked by default) → maps to `--parallel` flag

### Dialog Buttons
- **"Split"** button (OK action) — executes the split
- **Cancel** button

### Validation
- At least one file must be unchecked (otherwise no split happens — nothing moves to second commit)
- At least one file must be checked (otherwise original becomes empty — warn but allow)

### Data Structure
```kotlin
data class SplitSpec(
    val revision: Revision,
    val filePaths: List<FilePath>,   // Files for first commit (checked). Empty = all.
    val description: Description?,    // -m flag for first commit
    val parallel: Boolean             // --parallel flag
)
```

## Command Executor

### Interface addition (`CommandExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
fun split(
    revision: Revision = WorkingCopy,
    filePaths: List<FilePath> = emptyList(),
    description: Description? = null,
    parallel: Boolean = false
): CommandResult
```

### CLI implementation (`CliExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
override fun split(revision, filePaths, description, parallel) =
    execute(root, splitArgs(revision, filePaths, description, parallel))

internal fun splitArgs(
    revision: Revision,
    filePaths: List<FilePath>,
    description: Description?,
    parallel: Boolean
): List<String> = buildList {
    add("split")
    add("-r"); add(revision.toString())
    if (description != null) { add("-m"); add(description.actual) }
    if (parallel) add("--parallel")
    filePaths.forEach { add(it.relativeTo(root)) }
}
```

## Action Factory (`splitAction.kt`)

```kotlin
fun splitAction(project: Project, entry: LogEntry?) =
    nullAndDumbAwareAction(entry, "log.action.split", AllIcons.Actions.SplitVertically) {
        val repo = target.repo
        val changes = ChangeService.loadChanges(target)
        val dialog = SplitDialog(project, target, changes)
        if (!dialog.showAndGet()) return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        val spec = dialog.result ?: return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        repo.commandExecutor
            .createCommand { split(spec.revision, spec.filePaths, spec.description, spec.parallel) }
            .onSuccess { repo.invalidate(select = target.id) }
            .onFailureTellUser("log.action.split.error", project, log)
            .executeAsync()
    }
```

**Note**: Unlike squash where we need to load files async then show dialog, split loads files synchronously because the action already runs from the nullAndDumbAwareAction context (which is on EDT after user clicks). To avoid blocking EDT, wrap file loading in a background task or use the two-phase async pattern from squash.

## Refresh After Split

Call `repo.invalidate(select = target.id)`:
- The original change retains its change ID with the "kept" files
- The new child commit gets a new change ID
- Select the original change so the user can see the result

## File Structure

New files:
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/vcs/actions/splitAction.kt` — Action factory
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/split/SplitDialog.kt` — Dialog

Modified files:
- `CommandExecutor.kt` — Add `split()` method
- `CliExecutor.kt` — Implement `split()` + `splitArgs()`
- `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt` — Add split to context menu
- `JujutsuBundle.properties` — Add message keys

## Bundle Properties
```properties
log.action.split=Split...
log.action.split.tooltip=Split this change into two changes
log.action.split.error.title=Split Failed
log.action.split.error.message=Failed to split change:\n{0}
dialog.split.title=Split Change
dialog.split.source=Source
dialog.split.files=Files to keep in first commit
dialog.split.files.hint=Checked files stay in the original commit; unchecked files move to a new child
dialog.split.description=Description for first commit
dialog.split.parallel=Create parallel commits (siblings instead of parent/child)
dialog.split.validation.none.unchecked=Uncheck at least one file to split
dialog.split.button=Split
```

## Tests

- `CliExecutorSplitTest.kt` — Verify CLI arg construction for all flag combinations:
  - Basic split (revision only)
  - With file paths
  - With `-m` description
  - With `--parallel`
  - All combinations

- `JujutsuLogContextMenuActionsTest.kt` — Add split availability tests:
  - Immutable entries filtered out (single-select, `!immutable`)
  - Mutable entries pass through
  - Working copy mutable entries pass through
  - Multi-select returns null (single-select only)

## Design Rationale

**Checked = stays, unchecked = moves**: This matches `jj split`'s CLI semantics where filesets specify what stays. Users think "I want to keep these files here and move the rest out."

**No description for second commit**: `jj split` handles the second commit's description automatically (uses the original description or prompts). Adding a second text area would complicate the UI for a rare need.

**Parallel option**: Simple checkbox because it's a single flag. Advanced users who want parallel commits can toggle it; most users leave it unchecked.

**Shared FileSelectionPanel**: Reuses the same component as squash, avoiding duplicate checkbox tree infrastructure.

description:
Implement right-aligned refs in the separate Description column renderer.

## Current State

The plugin has two rendering modes:

### 1. Combined Mode (Default - Already Working ✅)
- Uses JujutsuGraphAndDescriptionRenderer
- Decorations (bookmarks, @ symbol) are already right-aligned (lines 371-409)
- Works by calculating decoration widths and painting them from right to left

### 2. Separate Columns Mode (NOT Working ❌)
When users enable separate columns:
- SeparateDescriptionCellRenderer (line 129) - only shows description text
- SeparateDecorationsCellRenderer (line 154) - shows @ and bookmarks left-aligned

## What's Remaining

### This Task (jj-idea-srz):
Implement right-aligned refs in the **separate** Description column. This requires:

1. **Merge decorations into Description column** - The separate Description renderer needs to include refs (currently they're in a separate Decorations column)

2. **Create custom renderer** - Replace SeparateDescriptionCellRenderer (which extends ColoredTableCellRenderer) with a custom implementation that:
   - Extends JPanel or similar (not ColoredTableCellRenderer)
   - Overrides paintComponent() to manually position elements
   - Paints description text left-aligned
   - Paints refs/decorations right-aligned
   - Handles truncation when content doesn't fit

3. **Width calculations** - Similar to JujutsuGraphAndDescriptionRenderer:
   - Calculate decoration widths (bookmarks + @ symbol)
   - Reserve space on the right
   - Truncate description if needed

4. **Use platform APIs if available** - If jj-idea-u29 is completed, use VcsLogLabelPainter for native rendering; otherwise fall back to current simple text approach

## Technical Challenge

ColoredTableCellRenderer only supports appending left-to-right, so custom painting is required for right alignment. The combined renderer already demonstrates this pattern (JujutsuGraphAndDescriptionRenderer lines 305-409).

## Files to Modify
- src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/log/JujutsuLogTableRenderers.kt (SeparateDescriptionCellRenderer)

title: Access internal rendering APIs (VcsLogLabelPainter, BookmarkIcon)
description:
## Goal

Access/implement platform-style rendering APIs similar to IntelliJ's VcsLogLabelPainter and BookmarkIcon for native-looking bookmark/reference rendering in the custom log UI.

## Investigation

### Platform APIs Found

1. **VcsLogLabelPainter** (`vcs-log-impl`): 
   - Internal API (`@ApiStatus.Internal`)
   - Handles rendering of VCS references and bookmarks with platform-native styling
   - Uses LabelPainter base class for actual painting
   - Manages ref grouping, colors, and layout

2. **BookmarkIcon** (`vcs-log-impl`):
   - Internal API (`@ApiStatus.Internal`)
   - Renders bookmark flag icon with proper scaling and theming
   - Color: `VersionControl.RefLabel.bookmarkBackground`
   - Draws as small flag/ribbon shape

3. **LabelPainter** (`vcs-log-impl`):
   - Internal API (`@ApiStatus.Internal`)
   - Base rendering class that paints reference labels
   - Handles compact vs. long presentation modes
   - Manages truncation, spacing, and icon rendering
   - Uses rounded rectangles for label backgrounds

### Availability

- Already accessible via transitive dependency: `intellij.platform.vcs.log.impl`
- Currently NOT imported or used in the plugin

### Decision

**Implement our own versions** instead of using internal APIs directly:

**Rationale:**
- Internal APIs can change/break between IntelliJ versions
- We need JJ-specific adaptations anyway
- Copying/adapting gives us full control
- Can still maintain platform-native look and feel

## Implementation Plan

1. Create `JujutsuBookmarkIcon` - Icon for rendering bookmark flags
   - Adapt rendering logic from platform's BookmarkIcon
   - Use platform colors from theme
   - Support proper scaling

2. Create `JujutsuLabelPainter` - Utility for painting reference labels
   - Adapt layout logic from platform's LabelPainter  
   - Support both compact and expanded modes
   - Handle truncation and spacing
   - Use rounded rectangle backgrounds like platform

3. Integration points:
   - Use in `JujutsuGraphAndDescriptionRenderer` for right-aligned bookmarks
   - Use in `SeparateDescriptionCellRenderer` for separate column mode
   - Apply consistent styling across the plugin

## Files to Create

- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/log/JujutsuBookmarkIcon.kt`
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/log/JujutsuLabelPainter.kt`

## Files to Modify

- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/log/JujutsuGraphAndDescriptionRenderer.kt`
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/log/JujutsuLogTableRenderers.kt`

description:
## Task: Implement Basic Custom VcsLogUi

**Goal**: Create a custom VcsLogUi implementation with table and graph rendering

**Progress**: Started skeleton implementation in `JujutsuCustomVcsLogUi.kt`

**Complexity Discovered**:

VcsLogUiEx interface requires implementing many methods:
- `getTable()` - Returns VcsLogGraphTable
- `getRefresher()` - Returns VisiblePackRefresher  
- `getId()` - Returns unique ID string
- `getVcsLog()` - Returns VcsLog facade
- `getDataPack()` - Returns current VisiblePack
- `getMainComponent()` - Returns main UI component
- `getProperties()` - Returns VcsLogUiProperties (still missing)
- And likely more...

**Challenges**:

1. **VcsLogGraphTable Creation**: Requires GraphTableModel, which needs:
   - VcsLogData
   - VisiblePackRefresher
   - Custom column configuration
   
2. **VcsLog Facade**: Need to implement/wrap VcsLog interface

3. **Properties Management**: VcsLogUiProperties for UI state

4. **Lifecycle Management**: Proper Disposable implementation

**Estimated Effort**: Much larger than initially thought - this is essentially reimplementing a significant portion of IntelliJ's VCS log UI.

**Recommendation**: Consider whether simpler approaches could achieve our goals:
- Enhanced column renderers with reflection for internal APIs?
- Custom column provider with workarounds for limitations?
- Accept some limitations to avoid this complexity?

**Status**: Blocked pending decision on whether to continue with full implementation or pursue alternative approaches.

title: Render refs/bookmarks with platform-style icons instead of colored text
description:
Replace the current colored text rendering of refs/bookmarks with fancy icons matching the platform's default Commit column appearance.

## Current Implementation

Custom Description column renders refs as colored text:
- Bold, smaller font
- Color from `VcsRef.type.backgroundColor`
- Functional but less polished than platform's version

Example: `description text  bookmark1 tag1 @`

## Platform's Approach

Default Commit column uses sophisticated icon-based rendering:
- **Bookmarks**: Small golden bookmark icon (path-based shape)
- **Tags**: Colored rounded rectangles with tag name
- **References**: Colored labels with ref type colors
- **Compact mode**: Icons only, no text
- **Tooltips**: Hover shows full ref details

## Why This Is Hard

All icon rendering infrastructure is **internal-only**:

### 1. VcsLogLabelPainter (internal)
From `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/VcsLogLabelPainter.kt`:
```kotlin
internal class VcsLogLabelPainter(
    private val logData: VcsLogData, 
    component: JComponent, 
    iconCache: LabelIconCache
) : LabelPainter(component, iconCache) {
    fun customizePainter(
        references: Collection<VcsRef>,
        bookmarks: Collection<VcsBookmarkRef>,
        // ...
    )
}
```

### 2. BookmarkIcon (internal)
From `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/BookmarkIcon.java`:
```java
public class BookmarkIcon implements Icon {
  private final Color BOOKMARK_REF_TYPE_COLOR = 
      JBColor.namedColor("VersionControl.RefLabel.bookmarkBackground",
                         new JBColor(0xf4af3d, 0xd9a343));
  
  @Override
  public void paintIcon(Component c, Graphics g, int iconX, int iconY) {
    // Draws bookmark path shape
    Path2D.Float path = new Path2D.Float();
    path.moveTo(x, y);
    // ... bookmark shape
    g2.fill(path);
  }
}
```

### 3. LabelPainter (internal)
From `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/LabelPainter.java`:
- Manages icon positioning and sizing
- Handles compact vs. expanded mode
- Calculates available width and truncation
- All methods are `internal` or package-private

### 4. LabelIcon (internal)
Renders colored rounded rectangles for ref labels.

## Possible Approaches (without custom log pane)

### 1. Copy/Reimplement Icons (high effort)
- Copy `BookmarkIcon`, `LabelIcon` source code
- Reimplement `LabelPainter` logic
- Maintain as platform updates
- **Risk**: Legal/licensing concerns, high maintenance

### 2. Reflection Access (fragile)
```kotlin
// Try to instantiate internal classes via reflection
val painterClass = Class.forName("com.intellij.vcs.log.ui.render.VcsLogLabelPainter")
// Very fragile, breaks with obfuscation/changes
```

### 3. Custom Icons (medium effort)
- Create our own bookmark/tag icon implementations
- Won't match platform exactly but close approximation
- Reasonable approach if custom log pane not pursued

## Proper Solution

**Requires custom VCS log pane** (see epic jj-idea-gyo):
- Implement custom renderer with access to internal classes
- Can use reflection or direct instantiation in custom UI
- Full control over rendering pipeline
- Can match platform appearance exactly

## Visual Comparison

**Current (colored text)**:
```
Fix bug in parser  main trunk @
```

**Desired (with icons)**:
```
Fix bug in parser  [📌 main] [📦 trunk] [@]
```
(where [📌] is golden bookmark icon, [📦] is colored rounded label, etc.)

## Investigation References

Platform files studied:
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/VcsLogLabelPainter.kt`
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/BookmarkIcon.java`
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/LabelPainter.java`
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/LabelIcon.java`
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/GraphCommitCellRenderer.kt`

## Dependency
Blocked by jj-idea-gyo (Custom VCS log pane implementation)

## Priority
P2 - Nice to have, current colored text is functional

title: Replace/hide default Commit (description) column
description:
Remove or hide the platform's default "Commit" column so only the custom Jujutsu Description column is visible, eliminating redundancy.

## Current Situation

Both columns are currently visible:
1. **Default "Commit" column** (platform):
   - Shows commit subject/description
   - Has fancy bookmark/tag icons
   - Cannot be hidden (not a dynamic column)
   - Always appears before custom columns

2. **Custom "Jujutsu.Description" column**:
   - Shows description with grey/italic for empty
   - Shows refs/bookmarks with colored text
   - Has tooltip with full description
   - Can be hidden by user

## Problem

- Duplicate information displayed
- User must manually hide custom column or live with redundancy
- Default column cannot be hidden via UI or API
- Default column always appears first, custom column comes after

## Why This Is Hard

The default "Commit" column is **hardcoded as non-dynamic**:

From `VcsLogDefaultColumn.kt`:
```kotlin
object Commit : VcsLogDefaultColumn<GraphCommitCell>(
    "Default.Subject", 
    VcsLogBundle.message("vcs.log.column.subject"), 
    false  // <-- isDynamic = false, cannot be hidden
)

companion object {
  // Root and Commit are always included
  private val defaultColumns = listOf(Root, Commit, Author, Date, Hash)
}
```

From `VcsLogColumnUtil.kt`:
```kotlin
fun getDynamicColumns(): List<VcsLogColumn<*>> {
  val columns: MutableList<VcsLogColumn<*>> = ArrayList()
  columns.addAll(getDefaultDynamicColumns())  // Only Author, Hash, Date
  columns.addAll(VcsLogCustomColumn.KEY.extensionList)
  return columns.filter { it.isDynamic }  // Commit filtered out here
}
```

**No public API exists** to:
- Change `isDynamic` property of default columns
- Remove columns from the default column list
- Override column manager behavior
- Programmatically hide specific columns

## Possible Workarounds (without custom log pane)

### 1. Reflection Hack (fragile)
```kotlin
// Access internal VcsLogColumnManager and remove Commit column
// Very fragile, breaks with platform updates
```

### 2. Override Default Column (might not work)
```kotlin
// Try registering a column with id "Default.Subject"
// May conflict with platform's registration
```

### 3. Accept Redundancy (current approach)
- Document that users should manually hide one column
- Not ideal UX but functional

## Proper Solution

**Requires custom VCS log pane** (see epic jj-idea-gyo):
- Implement custom `VcsLogUi` that excludes default Commit column
- Full control over column list and behavior
- Significant implementation effort

## Investigation References

Platform files studied:
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/table/column/VcsLogDefaultColumn.kt` (lines 37, 83-145)
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/table/column/VcsLogColumnUtil.kt` (lines 45-127)
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/table/column/VcsLogColumnManager.kt` (lines 30-56)

## Dependency
Blocked by jj-idea-gyo (Custom VCS log pane implementation)

## Priority
P2 - Annoying but not blocking, users can manually hide the unwanted column

title: [Epic] Custom VCS log pane implementation
description:
# Custom VCS Log Pane Implementation

Replace IntelliJ's default VCS log UI with a custom implementation to enable deep customization of the log view that's not possible with the current public API.

## Why Custom Implementation Needed

The IntelliJ VCS log framework has several limitations for plugins:

1. **Fixed default columns**: `Root` and `Commit` columns are hardcoded and cannot be hidden or reordered
2. **No column visibility control**: No public API to programmatically set column visibility (all column management APIs are `internal`)
3. **Limited rendering customization**: Cannot use platform's internal rendering infrastructure (`VcsLogLabelPainter`, `BookmarkIcon`, etc.)
4. **Column ordering restrictions**: Custom columns always appear after fixed platform columns

## Investigation Findings

### API Research (2025-12-21)

**VCS Log Tab Creation APIs**:
- `VcsLogContentUtil.openLogTab()` - Exists but requires already-created VcsLogUi
- `VcsLogManager.getInstance()`, `createLogUi()` - Not available in public SDK
- `VcsLogManager.openNewLogTab()` - Used internally by platform, not exposed
- Most tab creation methods are in internal classes not accessible to plugins

**Working Prototype Created**:
- Service: `JujutsuCustomLogTabManager`
- Action: `OpenJujutsuLogTabAction`
- Currently opens standard VCS log (uses our custom columns via extension points)
- Files: `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/JujutsuCustomLogTabManager.kt`

**Limitations with Standard Approach**:
- ✅ Custom columns work (Status, Change ID, Description, Committer)
- ❌ Default columns (Root, Commit) cannot be hidden
- ❌ Cannot control column ordering
- ❌ Cannot access internal rendering APIs

### Implementation Approach - Path B (Full Custom)

**Decision**: Proceed with full custom VcsLogUi implementation despite complexity

**Strategy**:
1. Study IntelliJ platform source for VcsLogUi patterns
2. Create custom VcsLogUiFactory implementation
3. Build custom table model and column manager
4. Use reflection or code copying to access internal rendering APIs
5. Manage lifecycle properly to avoid memory leaks

**Key Platform Classes to Study**:
- `VcsLogUiImpl` - Main log UI implementation
- `VcsLogGraphTable` - Table component
- `GraphTableModel` - Table data model
- `VcsLogColumnManager` - Column management (internal)
- `VcsLogLabelPainter`, `BookmarkIcon` - Rendering (internal)

## What Custom Implementation Would Enable

1. **Complete column control**:
   - Hide or replace default Commit column
   - Position custom columns first
   - Programmatic column visibility defaults

2. **Advanced rendering**:
   - Use platform's label painters and icons
   - Custom graph rendering
   - Right-aligned refs in description column

3. **Custom UI/UX**:
   - Different table layout
   - Custom row rendering
   - Additional UI controls

## Dependent Tasks

Tasks that require custom log pane:
- jj-idea-dtc: Render refs/bookmarks with platform-style icons
- jj-idea-n0o: Right-align references in Description column
- jj-idea-u4g: Replace/hide default Commit column

## Complexity Assessment

**Very High** - This is a significant undertaking:
- Need to understand and replicate substantial platform VCS log infrastructure
- Requires reflection or code copying to access internal APIs
- Maintenance burden as platform evolves
- Risk of breaking with platform updates

**Mitigation**:
- Start with basic feature parity
- Isolate internal API usage for easier updates
- Comprehensive testing
- Document all assumptions and workarounds

## Priority

P2 - High priority. Extension point approach cannot achieve required customizations.

title: Right-align references/bookmarks in Description column
description:
Currently, references (bookmarks, tags, @) are appended inline after the description text. For better visual alignment with the platform's default Commit column, these should be right-aligned within the column.

## Implementation Challenge

This requires custom painting beyond what `ColoredTableCellRenderer` provides:

1. **ColoredTableCellRenderer limitation**: Only supports left-aligned text appending
2. **Solution requires**: Override `paintComponent()` to manually position refs on the right
3. **Similar to platform approach**: Platform uses `VcsLogLabelPainter` with explicit x-coordinate positioning

## Technical Details

Platform's approach (from investigation):
- `GraphCommitCellRenderer.RealCommitRendererComponent.paintComponent()` calculates position:
  ```kotlin
  val start = max(graphWidth, (width - referencePainter.getSize().width)).toInt()
  referencePainter.paint(g2d, start, 0, height)
  ```
- Uses `LabelPainter` (internal API) which measures ref widths and paints at specific coordinates

## Custom Implementation Needed

Would require:
1. Custom `TableCellRenderer` extending `JPanel` or similar
2. Override `paintComponent()` to paint description left, refs right
3. Measure available width and ref widths to calculate positioning
4. Handle truncation when description + refs exceed column width

## Related Files
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/vcs/log/JujutsuVcsLogColumns.kt` (JujutsuDescriptionRenderer)
- Platform reference: `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/GraphCommitCellRenderer.kt`

## Dependency
May be easier to implement as part of custom VCS log pane implementation (see beads-XXX).

## Priority
P2 - Nice to have, current inline display is functional

title: Implement RepositoryLocation for remote repository integration
description:
Implement RepositoryLocation for integration with remote repository (GitHub, GitLab, etc).

Currently getChangedRepositoryPath() returns null. Should return proper RepositoryLocation for:
- Linking to remote repository in history view
- "Open in GitHub/GitLab" actions
- Remote diff viewing

**Location**: JujutsuFileRevision.kt line 44

**Implementation**:
- Determine if repo has remote configured
- Map file path to remote repository URL
- Return RepositoryLocation with remote URL

**Related**: jj-idea-4kg.8 (Open in remote)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Returns RepositoryLocation when remote is configured
- [ ] Null for local-only repositories
- [ ] Works with GitHub, GitLab, etc.
close_reason: Reverted and closing: RepositoryLocation is not needed. The 'Open in remote' feature (jj-idea-4kg.8) does not use RepositoryLocation at all — IntelliJ's Open on GitHub works through Git hosting service infrastructure (GitRepositoryManager, HostedGitRepositoriesManager), not through VcsFileRevision.getChangedRepositoryPath(). The only platform features that use getChangedRepositoryPath() are ShowAllAffectedGenericAction and CreatePatchFromDirectoryAction, both of which also require CommittedChangesProvider which we don't implement. This was premature abstraction with no consumer.

title: Localise all resources
Bookmark management UI
description:
**Priority**: HIGH (Phase 2 - Simple write operations)
**Effort**: 2-3 days

## Description
Create/delete/rename/move bookmarks (Jujutsu's version of branches).

## User Experience
- Bookmarks shown in log view
- Right-click change → "Create Bookmark Here"
- Right-click bookmark → "Delete Bookmark"
- Right-click bookmark → "Rename Bookmark"
- Right-click bookmark → "Move to Change..."

## Implementation
Add to `JujutsuCommandExecutor`:
```kotlin
fun bookmarkCreate(name: String, changeId: String): CommandResult
fun bookmarkDelete(name: String): CommandResult
fun bookmarkRename(oldName: String, newName: String): CommandResult
fun bookmarkSet(name: String, changeId: String): CommandResult
fun bookmarkList(): CommandResult
```

## Commands
- `jj bookmark create <name> -r <change-id>`
- `jj bookmark delete <name>`
- `jj bookmark rename <old> <new>`
- `jj bookmark set <name> -r <change-id>`
- `jj bookmark list`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create bookmark from context menu
- [ ] Delete bookmark from context menu
- [ ] Rename bookmark action
- [ ] Move bookmark to different change
- [ ] Bookmarks shown in log view
- [ ] Current bookmark highlighted
- [ ] Validation for bookmark names

## References
See ROADMAP.md Phase 2 - Simple Write Operations
design:
## Design Summary

### Scope
Core bookmark CRUD via log context menu and VCS menu/toolbar. Follow-ups for bookmark widget and interactive decorations tracked separately.

### Design Decisions
- **Create** uses `jj bookmark create` (fails if exists - clear error, not silently moving)
- **Move** uses `jj bookmark set -r <revision>` (create-or-update expected for move intent)
- **Multi-bookmark**: Submenu listing per-bookmark delete/rename actions
- **Confirmation**: Only delete prompts; create/rename/move are easily undoable

### Implementation

**CommandExecutor** (4 new methods):
- bookmarkCreate(name, revision)
- bookmarkDelete(name)
- bookmarkRename(oldName, newName)
- bookmarkSet(name, revision)

**CliExecutor**: Top-level testable arg builders (bookmarkCreateArgs, etc.) + override implementations.

**Action factories** (4 new files in vcs/actions/):
- bookmarkCreateAction(project, entry?) - prompts for name, creates at entry.id
- bookmarkDeleteAction(project, repo?, bookmark?) - confirms, deletes
- bookmarkRenameAction(project, repo?, bookmark?) - prompts new name
- bookmarkMoveAction(project, entry?) - loads bookmarks off EDT, popup chooser, sets

title: File ignoring support (.gitignore)
description:
**Priority**: MEDIUM (Phase 2 - Simple write operations)
**Effort**: 1-2 days

## Description
Support .gitignore for excluding files from VCS.

## User Experience
- Ignored files grayed out in tree
- Right-click → "Add to .gitignore"
- Right-click → "Remove from .gitignore"

## Implementation
```kotlin
class JujutsuIgnoredFileProvider {
    fun isIgnored(file: VirtualFile): Boolean {
        // Read .gitignore patterns
        // Check if file matches
    }
}
```

## Integration
- Use `ChangelistBuilder.processIgnoredFile(file)`
- Custom FileStatus for ignored files

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reads .gitignore patterns
- [ ] Ignored files grayed out
- [ ] "Add to .gitignore" action
- [ ] "Remove from .gitignore" action
- [ ] Respects .gitignore hierarchy
- [ ] Updates when .gitignore changes

## Note
Jujutsu uses Git's .gitignore format automatically.

## References
See ROADMAP.md Phase 2 - Simple Write Operations

title: Add Show History to working copy window
description: The working copy window menu should have the 'Show History' option. While there, make this menu as consistent as possible with the one for file changes from the commit detail pane.

title: Single click in working copy panel opens preview (UX decision needed)
description: Why: manual-tests.md line 149 says 'Single click does nothing' is marked as failing (❌). The working copy panel's UnifiedWorkingCopyPanel.kt has a MouseAdapter that opens the file in a preview tab on single click (lines 304-312). Whether this is the desired UX (like IntelliJ's built-in Changes view does preview-on-click) or undesired needs a design decision. If preview-on-click is correct, remove the ❌ from the manual test and mark it ✅. If it should do nothing, remove the MouseAdapter.

title: Select in Working Copy
description: Add a 'Working Copy' entry to the 'Select In' menu that appears when pressing option+F1. This should be present for all editors that represent changes.
notes: Implement SelectInTarget. The target should accept a file path, find the matching change in the working copy via stateModel.repositoryStates, fire stateModel.changeSelection, and focus the Changes tool window. JujutsuStateModel.changeSelection is a Notifier<ChangeKey> that the working copy panel already listens to. Register via com.intellij.selectInTarget extension point. The target should be visible only when the file has a local working copy change.

title: Squash requires a single parent
description: Squashing arbitrarily picks the first parent if the working copy is a merge. It should be more explicit, perhaps allowing the user to pick a parent.

title: Working copy window shows Git changes
JujutsuStateModel.repositoryStates is mis-modelled
description: JujutsuStateModel.repositoryStates is currently a set of log entries - one for each configured repository - representing the working copy of each repository. This has a confusing name and is difficult to work with. In most cases, we need to get the working copy log entry for a specific repo - so a map keyed by repo or repo name would make sense. We should also name this field more specifically - it is not 'repository states' - it is 'working copy by repo'.
close_reason: Closed

title: Nonlinear history
description: The history log is currently a table, which assumes that the history for a file is linear. In reality, the history could include merges and branches from those. This feature ensures that the history shows a log with a graph

title: Annotate previous revision submenu for merge
description: When annotating a merge commit, 'annotate previous revision' is ambiguous - as there could be several. In this situation, create a submenu for each revision. Ideally, include part of the description in the submenu, with the short change id if there is space, even better if it could be formatted.

title: Support multi-parent (merge) commits throughout the plugin
description: JJ supports merge commits natively, but several parts of the plugin assume a single parent for the working copy and for historical commits. This uber-issue tracks all multi-parent support work. Sub-issues cover: correct merge diffs (reverse-apply), atomic flip of tracker/diff/annotation base to @, and annotation previous-revision for merges. All sub-issues should be resolved before this can be closed.

title: Annotate previous revision broken for merge commits
description: When annotating a file and selecting 'Annotate Previous Revision', the 'previous' revision is determined by JujutsuFileAnnotation.getRevisions() ordering (by author timestamp). For a change that is the result of a merge, there may be multiple valid previous revisions (one per parent). Currently the code picks a single previous revision from the ordered list, which may not be ancestrally correct for merge commits. This is part of the broader multi-parent support work.

title: Atomically flip annotation, diff, and tracker base from @- to reverse-diffed @
description: When jj-idea-ayk7 (reverse-apply jj diff --git for auto-merged parent content) is complete, atomically switch all three systems to use the correct @ base: (1) LineStatusTracker base content, (2) diff screens, and (3) file annotations. Currently all three use @- (first parent) which is correct only for non-merge commits. After ayk7 lands, we can use the reverse-diffed @ content for all three, giving users true annotation of the working copy including local changes, correct gutter markers, and correct diffs for merges. These three changes must ship together so gutter markers, diffs, and annotations always use the same base.
close_reason: Closed

title: Log tree lines corrupt when filtered
description: When filtering the log, tree lines no longer join up. Sometimes this may be sensible, but JJ shows wiggly lines to indicate that there are elided revisions in between. Worse, when removing the filter, the lines are still corrupted until refreshing.

title: Add Jujutsu > Annotate action to working copy menu
notes: The standard Annotate action (id='Annotate') is already in Jujutsu.EditorGroup. For the working copy panel, fileChangeActionGroup() needs it added. The working copy panel's JujutsuChangesTree populates VIRTUAL_FILE via uiDataSnapshot so the standard Annotate action should resolve the file correctly. Add actionManager.getAction('Annotate') to fileChangeActionGroup() in its own group before the compare group, similar to how the editor group is structured.

title: Unify annotation and log tooltip via shared ChangeDetail interface
description: The annotation tooltip and log tooltip/details panel should reuse the same rendering code. Currently they diverge because the log uses LogEntry and the annotation uses AnnotationLine, and the two have different fields.
design:
## Approach

1. **Extend AnnotationLine** with fields currently missing vs LogEntry:
   - committer: VcsUser, committerTimestamp: Instant?
   - bookmarks: List<Bookmark>
   - isWorkingCopy, hasConflict, isEmpty, immutable: Boolean
   - repo: JujutsuRepository (already available in JujutsuFileAnnotation)
   Add these to the jj annotate template via commit.* fields. Bookmarks via commit.bookmarks().map(|b| b.name()).join(chr(1)) and split on chr(1). Status flags via if(commit.X(), 'true', 'false').

2. **Create shared interface ChangeDetail** (or similar):
   - id, commitId, description, bookmarks
   - author, authorTimestamp, committer, committerTimestamp
   - isWorkingCopy, hasConflict, isEmpty, isDivergent, immutable
   Both LogEntry and AnnotationLine implement this.

3. **Move appendSummaryAndStatuses et al.** to work with ChangeDetail instead of LogEntry.

4. **Annotation tooltip** then calls appendSummaryAndStatuses(line) + author/date + description, identical to log tooltip.

## Note on repo
AnnotationLine doesn't currently hold a JujutsuRepository. It's available in JujutsuFileAnnotation at construction time — either add it to AnnotationLine or pass it separately into getHtmlTooltip().
close_reason: Closed

title: Show coloured repo icon in working copy
description: The working copy window shows a change tree for the working copy. Bits of the path in here appear as folders. Where these path elements are repository roots, it would be great to display with the repo icon, appropriately coloured for the repo, consistent with colouring for repo used elsewhere in the plugin.

title: Split bookmark move dialog into forward/sideways
description: The 'move bookmark' dialog contains a list of bookmarks that could be moved, in no particular order. It would be good to split this into two sections: the first for bookmarks that would actually move forward and the second for those that would move backwards or sideways. Even better if the icons could distinguish these scenarios. While here, introduce a margin on the table in this dialog, particularly on the left-hand side.

title: Post-delete notification with Push Now action
description: After successfully deleting a local bookmark that was tracking a remote, show a balloon notification: 'Bookmark main deleted. This deletion will be pushed to origin on next push.' with a 'Push Now' action button that triggers the push immediately. This teaches the jj two-step delete workflow (delete local → push deletion) rather than hiding it. Use JujutsuNotifications for the balloon.

title: Show pending bookmark deletions in push dialog
description: The push dialog should display bookmarks pending deletion with a visual deletion indicator (red cross, strikethrough, etc). Currently deletions are pushed silently with no preview. Changes needed: (1) Parse 'Delete bookmark X from <hash>' lines from dry-run output in gitRemoteActions.kt (currently only 'Move sideways/backward' is parsed), (2) Show these in the push dialog alongside bookmarks being pushed, (3) Optionally allow users to exclude specific deletions from the push. Depends on the pending deletion data model work. Related to jj-idea-7n98 (preview push).

title: Track pending bookmark deletions in data model and log
description: After `jj bookmark delete`, jj marks the bookmark as present=false — a pending deletion that will be pushed to the remote on next push. Currently the plugin filters these out entirely (CliLogService bookmarkListTemplate and GitPushDialog LOCAL_BOOKMARK_TEMPLATE both skip present=false). Changes needed: (1) Add a `deleted` field to the Bookmark data class, (2) Stop filtering present=false bookmarks in CliLogService, (3) Render pending-deletion bookmarks in the log with a visual indicator (strikethrough, red cross, or similar) so users can see that a deletion is staged. Related to jj-idea-b1d8 (distinguishing local/remote bookmark state) and jj-idea-rskx (bookmark icons).

title: Add bookmark forget action
description: Add `jj bookmark forget` to CommandExecutor/CliExecutor and a 'Forget' context menu action on local bookmarks. Forget removes the bookmark locally without creating a pending remote deletion — it simply erases jj's knowledge of the bookmark. On next fetch, a forgotten remote bookmark will reappear. Confirmation dialog should explain this distinction clearly: 'This removes the bookmark locally. It will NOT be deleted from the remote and will reappear on next fetch.' This is distinct from delete (which creates a pending remote deletion) and untrack (which stops syncing but keeps both sides).

title: Correct merge diff: reverse-apply jj diff --git for auto-merged parent content
description:
When the working copy (or any commit) is a merge, the 'before' content for diffs should be JJ's auto-merged parent tree, not any single parent's content. The first-parent approach (jj-idea-j1lc) prevents the crash but shows wrong diffs when a file was modified in multiple parents.

## Problem
JJ computes the auto-merged tree internally for `jj diff -r <merge>` but does not expose it via `jj file show` or any template. There is no JJ command that outputs auto-merged file content directly. The template system (TreeDiffEntry, TreeEntry) has no .content() method.

## Approach: Reverse-apply `jj diff --git`
For merge commits, use `jj diff --git -r <rev> -- <file>` to get the authoritative diff (which correctly computes against the auto-merged tree), then reconstruct the 'before' content by reverse-applying the unified diff to the current file content.

### Algorithm
1. Read current file content (the 'after') — from disk for working copy, or `jj file show -r <rev>` for historical
2. Run `jj diff --git -r <rev> -- <file>`
3. Parse the unified diff hunks
4. Walk the after content line-by-line, processing hunks:
   - Lines outside hunks: include as-is
   - `+` lines (present in after only): skip
   - `-` lines (present in before only): insert
   - Context lines: include as-is
5. Result is the before content

### Edge cases to handle
- Binary files (git diff shows 'Binary files differ')
- Added files (before is empty, no reverse needed)
- Deleted files (after is empty)
- Empty diff (file unchanged — shouldn't appear in status but handle gracefully)
- Files with no trailing newline

### Implementation outline
- Add `CommandExecutor.diffGitFile(revision: Revision, filePath: FilePath)` → runs `jj diff --git -r <rev> -- <file>`
- Add `GitDiffReverseApplier` utility: (afterContent: String, gitDiff: String) → beforeContent: String
- Modify `JujutsuContentRevision.getContent()`: detect merge parent marker, use reverse-apply path instead of `jj file show`
- Or: add `JujutsuRepository.mergeParentContent(filePath)` encapsulating the logic

### Files to modify
- `jj/cli/CliExecutor.kt` — add diffGitFile method
- `jj/CommandExecutor.kt` — add interface method
- `jj/JujutsuRepository.kt` — modify content retrieval for merge parents
- `jj/util/GitDiffReverseApplier.kt` — new file: parse + reverse-apply
- `vcs/changes/JujutsuChangeProvider.kt` — use merge-aware content
- `vcs/diff/JujutsuDiffProvider.kt` — use merge-aware content

### Scope
This also fixes the same bug for historical merge commits — `ChangeService.convertToChange()` uses `entry.parentIds.firstOrNull()` which has the same first-parent limitation.

### Research notes
- `jj diff --help`: 'If a revision is a merge commit, this shows changes from the automatic merge of the contents of all of its parents to the contents of the revision itself.'
- `jj status --help`: shows changes 'compared to the merged parents'
- git4idea doesn't have this problem — git's working copy is not a commit, HEAD always resolves to one commit
- JJ template TreeDiffEntry has .source()/.target() → TreeEntry, but TreeEntry has no .content() — can't extract file content via templates
- `jj file show -r @-` fails for merges because @- resolves to multiple commits
- The existing diffGit(revision) method in CommandExecutor runs `jj diff --git -r <rev>` (whole revision); need a per-file variant
close_reason: Implemented GitDiffReverseApplier, MergeParentOf revision type, diffGitFile command. workingCopyParent() now returns MergeParentOf for merge WCs; JujutsuContentRevision.getContent() reverse-applies the diff. ChangeService uses MergeParentOf for historical merge commits. JujutsuDiffProvider uses JujutsuMergeParentRevisionNumber to carry merge context through IntelliJ's revision number roundtrip.

title: Render issue tracker links in commit descriptions (GitHub #15)
description:
Integrate with com.intellij.openapi.vcs.IssueNavigationConfiguration so patterns like JIRA-123 in commit descriptions render as clickable links.

The historical (before) side cannot be annotated with DiffContentInfo.commitId without an extra async jj call to resolve the Revision to a CommitId.
